{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_186b9dc2318849059c25d092","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/**\n2\t * Authorization middleware for fine-grained access protection for state-changing operations\n8\t * - Rate limit tier enforcement\n9\t * - Admin-only operations protection\n10\t * - Data access validation (user-scoped data isolation)\n11\t * - Scope restriction helpers for filtering user data\n12\t */\n13\t\n14\timport type { Context, MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\n15\timport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\n16\timport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\n17\timport { type ApiKeyScope, type AuthContext, getAuthContext } from \"./authentication.js\";\n18\timport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n19\t\n20\t// Re-export RBAC types for compatibility\n21\texport type { PermissionAction, ResourceType, ResourcePolicy, AuthorizationResult };\n22\t\n23\t// ============================================================================\n24\t// Types\n25\t// ============================================================================\n26\t\n27\t/**\n28\t * Permission action types for resource-based access control.\n29\t */\n30\texport type PermissionAction = \"create\" | \"read\" | \"update\" | \"delete\" | \"admin\";\n31\t\n32\t/**\n33\t * Resource types that can be protected.\n34\t */\n35\texport type ResourceType =\n36\t  | \"trip\"\n37\t  | \"subscription\"\n38\t  | \"context\"\n39\t  | \"commute\"\n40\t  | \"journal\"\n41\t  | \"alert\"\n42\t  | \"equipment\"\n43\t  | \"prediction\"\n44\t  | \"admin\";\n45\t\n46\t/**\n47\t * Resource access policy - defines who can access what.\n48\t */\n49\texport interface ResourcePolicy {\n50\t  /** Resource type */\n51\t  resourceType: ResourceType;\n52\t  /** Required action */\n53\t  action: PermissionAction;\n54\t  /** Whether admin scope bypasses ownership checks */\n55\t  adminBypass?: boolean;\n56\t  /** Custom access check function */\n57\t  customCheck?: (context: Context, auth: AuthContext) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;\n58\t}\n59\t\n60\t/**\n61\t * Authorization result with detailed reason for denial.\n62\t */\n63\texport interface AuthorizationResult {\n64\t  /** Whether access is granted */\n65\t  allowed: boolean;\n66\t  /** Reason for denial (if denied) */\n67\t  reason?: string;\n68\t  /** Required scope/permission (if denied) */\n69\t  required?: string;\n70\t}\n71\t\n72\t// ============================================================================\n73\t// Resource ID Extraction Utilities\n74\t// ============================================================================\n75\t\n76\t/**\n77\t * Extract resource owner ID from various resource types.\n78\t * This validates that the authenticated user owns the resource being accessed.\n79\t */\n80\texport interface ResourceOwner {\n81\t  /** Owner identifier (e.g., subscription endpoint, trip ID with embedded user info) */\n82\t  ownerId: string;\n83\t  /** Whether the resource is public */\n84\t  isPublic: boolean;\n85\t}\n86\t\n87\t/**\n88\t * Extract owner identifier from a push subscription endpoint.\n89\t * The endpoint itself serves as the owner identifier since it's unique per device.\n90\t */\n91\tfunction _extractSubscriptionOwner(endpoint: string): ResourceOwner {\n92\t  return { ownerId: endpoint, isPublic: false };\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/**\n96\t * Extract owner identifier from a trip ID.\n97\t * For manual trips, the trip ID contains a timestamp-based component.\n98\t * For API-tracked trips, ownership should be validated via session.\n99\t */\n100\tfunction _extractTripOwner(tripId: string): ResourceOwner {\n101\t  // Trip IDs are UUIDs - ownership is validated via database lookup\n102\t  // In a real system, trips would have a userId field\n103\t  return { ownerId: tripId, isPublic: false };\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\t/**\n107\t * Extract owner from a commute ID.\n108\t */\n109\tfunction _extractCommuteOwner(commuteId: string): ResourceOwner {\n110\t  return { ownerId: commuteId, isPublic: false };\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// ============================================================================\n114\t// Authorization Middleware\n115\t// ============================================================================\n116\t\n117\t/**\n118\t * Verify that the authenticated user has access to a specific resource.\n119\t * Used for operations on specific user-owned resources (trips, subscriptions, etc.).\n120\t */\n121\texport function requireResourceAccess(\n122\t  resourceType: ResourceType,\n123\t  action: PermissionAction,\n124\t  options: {\n125\t    /** Admin scope bypasses ownership checks */\n126\t    adminBypass?: boolean;\n127\t    /** Custom access check function */\n128\t    customCheck?: (context: Context, auth: AuthContext) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;\n129\t    /** Resource ID parameter name (for extracting from route params) */\n130\t    resourceIdParam?: string;\n131\t  } = {}\n132\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n133\t  const { adminBypass = true, customCheck } = options;\n134\t\n135\t  return async (c, next) => {\n136\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n137\t\n138\t    // Must be authenticated\n139\t    if (!auth) {\n140\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n141\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    // Admin bypass check\n145\t    if (adminBypass && auth.scope === \"admin\") {\n146\t      logger.info(\"Admin bypass for resource access\", {\n147\t        keyId: auth.keyId,\n148\t        resourceType,\n149\t        action,\n150\t      });\n151\t      return next();\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    // Scope-based authorization check\n155\t    const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n156\t    const requiredScopeForAction: Record<PermissionAction, ApiKeyScope> = {\n157\t      create: \"write\",\n158\t      read: \"read\",\n159\t      update: \"write\",\n160\t      delete: \"write\",\n161\t      admin: \"admin\",\n162\t    };\n163\t\n164\t    const requiredScope = requiredScopeForAction[action];\n165\t    if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < scopeLevels[requiredScope]) {\n166\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n167\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n168\t        message: `Insufficient permissions. Required scope: ${requiredScope}`,\n169\t      });\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    // Custom access check if provided\n173\t    if (customCheck) {\n174\t      const hasAccess = await customCheck(c, auth);\n175\t      if (!hasAccess) {\n176\t        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n177\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n178\t          message: \"Access denied to this resource\",\n179\t        });\n180\t      }\n181\t    }\n182\t\n183\t    return next();\n184\t  };\n185\t}\n186\t\n187\t/**\n188\t * Require admin scope for privileged operations.\n189\t * Use for operations that affect system configuration or other users.\n190\t */\n191\texport function requireAdmin(): MiddlewareHandler {\n192\t  return async (c, next) => {\n193\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n194\t\n195\t    if (!auth) {\n196\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin\", \"admin\");\n197\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n198\t    }\n199\t\n200\t    if (auth.scope !== \"admin\") {\n201\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin\", \"admin\");\n202\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n203\t        message: \"Admin privileges required\",\n204\t      });\n205\t    }\n206\t\n207\t    logger.info(\"Admin operation authorized\", { keyId: auth.keyId });\n208\t    return next();\n209\t  };\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t/**\n213\t * Require write scope for state-changing operations.\n214\t * Use as a simpler alternative to requireResourceAccess for general write operations.\n215\t */\n216\texport function requireWrite(): MiddlewareHandler {\n217\t  return async (c, next) => {\n218\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n219\t\n220\t    if (!auth) {\n221\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"api\", \"write\");\n222\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n223\t    }\n224\t\n225\t    const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n226\t    if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < 2) {\n227\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"api\", \"write\");\n228\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n229\t        message: \"Write permissions required\",\n230\t      });\n231\t    }\n232\t\n233\t    return next();\n234\t  };\n235\t}\n236\t\n237\t/**\n238\t * Optional authorization - attaches auth context if present but doesn't require it.\n239\t * Use for endpoints that have enhanced features for authenticated users.\n240\t *\n241\t * Note: This is already provided by authentication.ts but re-exported for convenience.\n242\t */\n243\texport { optionalAuth, isAuthenticated } from \"./authentication.js\";\n244\t\n245\t/**\n246\t * Rate limit tier enforcement.\n247\t * Ensures API key rate limits are respected based on assigned tier.\n248\t */\n249\texport function enforceRateLimitTier(maxTier?: number): MiddlewareHandler {\n250\t  return async (c, next) => {\n251\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n252\t\n253\t    if (!auth) {\n254\t      // Unauthenticated requests use default rate limiting (middleware level)\n255\t      return next();\n256\t    }\n257\t\n258\t    // Check if the API key's rate limit tier is within bounds\n259\t    if (maxTier !== undefined && auth.rateLimitTier > maxTier) {\n260\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rate_limit\", \"tier_exceeded\");\n261\t      throw new HTTPException(429, {\n262\t        message: \"Rate limit tier exceeded for this operation\",\n263\t      });\n264\t    }\n265\t\n266\t    return next();\n267\t  };\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// ============================================================================\n271\t// Cross-Origin Protection for State Changes\n272\t// ============================================================================\n273\t\n274\t/**\n275\t * Verify same-origin for state-changing operations.\n276\t * Prevents CSRF attacks by validating the Origin header for POST/DELETE/PATCH.\n277\t */\n278\texport function requireSameOrigin(): MiddlewareHandler {\n279\t  return async (c, next) => {\n280\t    const method = c.req.method;\n281\t\n282\t    // Only apply to state-changing methods\n283\t    if (![\"POST\", \"DELETE\", \"PATCH\", \"PUT\"].includes(method)) {\n284\t      return next();\n285\t    }\n286\t\n287\t    const origin = c.req.header(\"Origin\");\n288\t    const host = c.req.header(\"Host\");\n289\t    const referer = c.req.header(\"Referer\");\n290\t\n291\t    // Skip for API calls with proper authentication (API key or session)\n292\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n293\t    if (auth && (auth.authMethod === \"api_key\" || auth.authMethod === \"session\")) {\n294\t      return next();\n295\t    }\n296\t\n297\t    // For browser-based requests without auth, check Origin/Referer\n298\t    if (!origin && !referer) {\n299\t      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"missing_origin_referer\");\n300\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n301\t        message: \"Origin or Referer header required for state-changing operations\",\n302\t      });\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    // Validate Origin matches Host (if Origin is present)\n306\t    if (origin && host) {\n307\t      const originHost = new URL(origin).host;\n308\t      if (originHost !== host) {\n309\t        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"origin_mismatch\");\n310\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n311\t          message: \"Cross-origin requests not allowed for state-changing operations\",\n312\t        });\n313\t      }\n314\t    }\n315\t\n316\t    // Validate Referer matches Host (if Referer is present and no Origin)\n317\t    if (referer && host && !origin) {\n318\t      const refererHost = new URL(referer).host;\n319\t      if (refererHost !== host) {\n320\t        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"referer_mismatch\");\n321\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n322\t          message: \"Cross-origin requests not allowed for state-changing operations\",\n323\t        });\n324\t      }\n325\t    }\n326\t\n327\t    return next();\n328\t  };\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// ============================================================================\n332\t// Data Access Validation\n333\t// ============================================================================\n334\t\n335\t/**\n336\t * Validate that a request only accesses data the user is allowed to see.\n337\t * Used for queries that might return user-scoped data.\n338\t */\n339\texport function validateDataAccess(resourceType: ResourceType): MiddlewareHandler {\n340\t  return async (c, next) => {\n341\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n342\t\n343\t    // For authenticated users, ensure they can only access their own data\n344\t    if (auth) {\n345\t      // Check query parameters for user-scoped filters\n346\t      const userId = c.req.query(\"userId\");\n347\t      const endpoint = c.req.query(\"endpoint\");\n348\t\n349\t      // If trying to access another user's data, require admin scope\n350\t      if ((userId || endpoint) && auth.scope !== \"admin\") {\n351\t        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, \"read\");\n352\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n353\t          message: \"Access to other users' data requires admin privileges\",\n354\t        });\n355\t      }\n356\t    }\n357\t\n358\t    return next();\n359\t  };\n360\t}\n361\t\n362\t// ============================================================================\n363\t// Authorization Check Utilities\n364\t// ============================================================================\n365\t\n366\t/**\n367\t * Check if the current context has authorization for a specific action.\n368\t * Returns an AuthorizationResult with details.\n369\t */\n370\texport function checkAuthorization(\n371\t  c: Context,\n372\t  resourceType: ResourceType,\n373\t  action: PermissionAction\n374\t): AuthorizationResult {\n375\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n376\t\n377\t  if (!auth) {\n378\t    return {\n379\t      allowed: false,\n380\t      reason: \"Not authenticated\",\n381\t      required: \"Authentication required\",\n382\t    };\n383\t  }\n384\t\n385\t  const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n386\t  const requiredScopeForAction: Record<PermissionAction, ApiKeyScope> = {\n387\t    create: \"write\",\n388\t    read: \"read\",\n389\t    update: \"write\",\n390\t    delete: \"write\",\n391\t    admin: \"admin\",\n392\t  };\n393\t\n394\t  const requiredScope = requiredScopeForAction[action];\n395\t  if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < scopeLevels[requiredScope]) {\n396\t    return {\n397\t      allowed: false,\n398\t      reason: \"Insufficient permissions\",\n399\t      required: requiredScope,\n400\t    };\n401\t  }\n402\t\n403\t  return { allowed: true };\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t/**\n407\t * Require MFA verification for sensitive operations.\n408\t * Use for operations that should require multi-factor authentication.\n409\t */\n410\texport function requireMfa(): MiddlewareHandler {\n411\t  return async (c, next) => {\n412\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n413\t\n414\t    if (!auth) {\n415\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"mfa\", \"required\");\n416\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n417\t    }\n418\t\n419\t    // Check if MFA was verified for this session\n420\t    if (!auth.mfaVerified) {\n421\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"mfa\", \"required\");\n422\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n423\t        message: \"Multi-factor authentication required for this operation\",\n424\t      });\n425\t    }\n426\t\n427\t    logger.info(\"MFA-verified operation authorized\", { keyId: auth.keyId });\n428\t    return next();\n429\t  };\n430\t}\n431\t\n432\t/**\n433\t * Audit log wrapper for privileged operations.\n434\t * Logs all access to sensitive resources for compliance.\n435\t */\n436\texport function auditLogAccess(\n437\t  resourceType: ResourceType,\n438\t  action: PermissionAction\n439\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n440\t  return async (c, next) => {\n441\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n442\t    const clientIp =\n443\t      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n444\t      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n445\t      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n446\t      \"unknown\";\n447\t\n448\t    // Log the attempt\n449\t    logger.info(\"Privileged operation attempt\", {\n450\t      keyId: auth?.keyId,\n451\t      resourceType,\n452\t      action,\n453\t      path: c.req.path,\n454\t      method: c.req.method,\n455\t      clientIp,\n456\t      authenticated: !!auth,\n457\t    });\n458\t\n459\t    // Execute the operation\n460\t    await next();\n461\t\n462\t    // Log the result\n463\t    const status = c.res.status;\n464\t    const success = status >= 200 && status < 300;\n465\t\n466\t    logger.info(\"Privileged operation completed\", {\n467\t      keyId: auth?.keyId,\n468\t      resourceType,\n469\t      action,\n470\t      path: c.req.path,\n471\t      status,\n472\t      success,\n473\t      clientIp,\n474\t    });\n475\t  };\n476\t}\n477\t\n478\t// ============================================================================\n479\t// Scope Restriction Helpers\n480\t// ============================================================================\n481\t\n482\t/**\n483\t * Get the owner ID for an authenticated user, or \"anonymous\" for unauthenticated.\n484\t * This is used to scope database queries to the user's own data.\n485\t *\n486\t * @param c - Hono context\n487\t * @param allowAdminAll - If true, admins get undefined (no filter), otherwise they also get their own keyId\n488\t * @returns Owner ID to use in queries, or undefined for admins to see all data\n489\t */\n490\texport function getOwnerIdForAuthContext(\n491\t  c: Context,\n492\t  allowAdminAll: boolean = true\n493\t): string | undefined {\n494\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n495\t\n496\t  // Unauthenticated users get anonymous\n497\t  if (!auth) {\n498\t    return \"anonymous\";\n499\t  }\n500\t\n501\t  // Admins can see all data if allowAdminAll is true\n502\t  if (allowAdminAll && auth.role === \"admin\") {\n503\t    return undefined; // No filter - return all data\n504\t  }\n505\t\n506\t  // Regular authenticated users get their own keyId\n507\t  return auth.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n508\t}\n509\t\n510\t/**\n511\t * Apply ownership scope to query parameters.\n512\t * Ensures non-admin users can only query their own resources.\n513\t *\n514\t * @param c - Hono context\n515\t * @param params - Existing query parameters\n516\t * @returns Parameters with ownership filter applied\n517\t */\n518\texport function applyOwnershipScope<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n519\t  c: Context,\n520\t  params: T\n521\t): T & { ownerId?: string } {\n522\t  const ownerId = getOwnerIdForAuthContext(c, true);\n523\t\n524\t  if (ownerId === undefined) {\n525\t    // Admin user - no ownership filter\n526\t    return params as T & { ownerId?: string };\n527\t  }\n528\t\n529\t  // Non-admin user - add ownership filter\n530\t  return { ...params, ownerId };\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t/**\n534\t * Require ownership of a resource for access.\n535\t * This is a helper that validates ownership before allowing access.\n536\t *\n537\t * @param c - Hono context\n538\t * @param resourceOwnerId - Owner ID of the resource being accessed\n539\t * @returns true if access is allowed, throws HTTPException otherwise\n540\t */\n541\texport function requireResourceOwnership(c: Context, resourceOwnerId: string): boolean {\n542\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n543\t\n544\t  // Admins bypass ownership checks\n545\t  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n546\t    return true;\n547\t  }\n548\t\n549\t  // Check if the user owns the resource\n550\t  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n551\t\n552\t  if (userOwnerId !== resourceOwnerId && resourceOwnerId !== \"anonymous\") {\n553\t    securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"resource\", \"ownership\");\n554\t    throw new HTTPException(403, {\n555\t      message: \"Access denied: you do not own this resource\",\n556\t    });\n557\t  }\n558\t\n559\t  return true;\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\t/**\n563\t * Scope options for database queries.\n564\t */\n565\texport interface ScopeOptions {\n566\t  /** Owner ID to filter by (undefined = no filter, return all) */\n567\t  ownerId?: string;\n568\t  /** Additional filter criteria */\n569\t  filters?: Record<string, unknown>;\n570\t  /** Limit on number of results */\n571\t  limit?: number;\n572\t  /** Offset for pagination */\n573\t  offset?: number;\n574\t}\n575\t\n576\t/**\n577\t * Create scoped query options for database operations.\n578\t * Automatically applies ownership filtering based on authentication.\n579\t *\n580\t * @param c - Hono context\n581\t * @param baseOptions - Base query options\n582\t * @returns Scoped options with ownership filter applied\n583\t */\n584\texport function createScopedQuery<T extends ScopeOptions>(c: Context, baseOptions: T = {} as T): T {\n585\t  const ownerId = getOwnerIdForAuthContext(c, true);\n586\t\n587\t  if (ownerId === undefined) {\n588\t    // Admin - return all data without ownership filter\n589\t    return baseOptions;\n590\t  }\n591\t\n592\t  // Non-admin - apply ownership filter\n593\t  return {\n594\t    ...baseOptions,\n595\t    ownerId,\n596\t  } as T;\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t/**\n600\t * Check if the current user can access another user's data.\n601\t * Returns true if the user is an admin or if the targetUserId matches their own.\n602\t *\n603\t * @param c - Hono context\n604\t * @param targetUserId - The user ID being accessed\n605\t * @returns true if access is allowed\n606\t */\n607\texport function canAccessUserData(c: Context, targetUserId: string): boolean {\n608\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n609\t\n610\t  // Admins can access any user's data\n611\t  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n612\t    return true;\n613\t  }\n614\t\n615\t  // Users can only access their own data\n616\t  return auth?.keyId === targetUserId;\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t/**\n620\t * Filter a response to include only user-scoped data.\n621\t * Removes sensitive information and ensures users only see their own data.\n622\t *\n623\t * @param c - Hono context\n624\t * @param data - Data to filter\n625\t * @param ownerField - Field name that contains the owner ID\n626\t * @returns Filtered data\n627\t */\n628\texport function filterUserScopedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n629\t  c: Context,\n630\t  data: T[],\n631\t  ownerField: string = \"ownerId\"\n632\t): T[] {\n633\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n634\t\n635\t  // Admins see all data\n636\t  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n637\t    return data;\n638\t  }\n639\t\n640\t  // Non-admins only see their own data\n641\t  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n642\t\n643\t  return data.filter((item) => {\n644\t    const ownerId = item[ownerField] as string | undefined;\n645\t    return ownerId === userOwnerId || ownerId === \"anonymous\";\n646\t  });\n647\t}\n648\t\n649\t/**\n650\t * Validate that a list request is properly scoped.\n651\t * Throws an error if a non-admin user tries to request another user's data.\n652\t *\n653\t * @param c - Hono context\n654\t * @param requestedOwnerId - Owner ID from request (if any)\n655\t * @throws HTTPException if access is denied\n656\t */\n657\texport function validateListScope(c: Context, requestedOwnerId?: string): void {\n658\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n659\t\n660\t  // If no specific owner requested, this is fine (will be filtered by applyOwnershipScope)\n661\t  if (!requestedOwnerId) {\n662\t    return;\n663\t  }\n664\t\n665\t  // Admins can request any owner's data\n666\t  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n667\t    return;\n668\t  }\n669\t\n670\t  // Non-admins can only request their own data\n671\t  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n672\t\n673\t  if (requestedOwnerId !== userOwnerId) {\n674\t    securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"list\", \"scope\");\n675\t    throw new HTTPException(403, {\n676\t      message: \"Access denied: you can only view your own data\",\n677\t    });\n678\t  }\n679\t}\n680\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c93cc6db-9604-447b-bedb-ccf0fc966d30","uuid":"4d2d4f35-ffec-497e-ba5f-d1e5452ceefd","timestamp":"2026-05-09T04:04:39.423Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/packages/server/src/middleware/authorization.ts","content":"/**\n * Authorization middleware for fine-grained access control.\n *\n * Provides:\n * - Resource-based access control (ownership verification)\n * - Action-based permissions (create, read, update, delete)\n * - Cross-origin request protection for state-changing operations\n * - Rate limit tier enforcement\n * - Admin-only operations protection\n * - Data access validation (user-scoped data isolation)\n * - Scope restriction helpers for filtering user data\n */\n\nimport type { Context, MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\nimport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport { type ApiKeyScope, type AuthContext, getAuthContext } from \"./authentication.js\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n// Re-export RBAC types for compatibility\nexport type { PermissionAction, ResourceType, ResourcePolicy, AuthorizationResult };\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Types\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Permission action types for resource-based access control.\n */\nexport type PermissionAction = \"create\" | \"read\" | \"update\" | \"delete\" | \"admin\";\n\n/**\n * Resource types that can be protected.\n */\nexport type ResourceType =\n  | \"trip\"\n  | \"subscription\"\n  | \"context\"\n  | \"commute\"\n  | \"journal\"\n  | \"alert\"\n  | \"equipment\"\n  | \"prediction\"\n  | \"admin\";\n\n/**\n * Resource access policy - defines who can access what.\n */\nexport interface ResourcePolicy {\n  /** Resource type */\n  resourceType: ResourceType;\n  /** Required action */\n  action: PermissionAction;\n  /** Whether admin scope bypasses ownership checks */\n  adminBypass?: boolean;\n  /** Custom access check function */\n  customCheck?: (context: Context, auth: AuthContext) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Authorization result with detailed reason for denial.\n */\nexport interface AuthorizationResult {\n  /** Whether access is granted */\n  allowed: boolean;\n  /** Reason for denial (if denied) */\n  reason?: string;\n  /** Required scope/permission (if denied) */\n  required?: string;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Resource ID Extraction Utilities\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Extract resource owner ID from various resource types.\n * This validates that the authenticated user owns the resource being accessed.\n */\nexport interface ResourceOwner {\n  /** Owner identifier (e.g., subscription endpoint, trip ID with embedded user info) */\n  ownerId: string;\n  /** Whether the resource is public */\n  isPublic: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract owner identifier from a push subscription endpoint.\n * The endpoint itself serves as the owner identifier since it's unique per device.\n */\nfunction _extractSubscriptionOwner(endpoint: string): ResourceOwner {\n  return { ownerId: endpoint, isPublic: false };\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract owner identifier from a trip ID.\n * For manual trips, the trip ID contains a timestamp-based component.\n * For API-tracked trips, ownership should be validated via session.\n */\nfunction _extractTripOwner(tripId: string): ResourceOwner {\n  // Trip IDs are UUIDs - ownership is validated via database lookup\n  // In a real system, trips would have a userId field\n  return { ownerId: tripId, isPublic: false };\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract owner from a commute ID.\n */\nfunction _extractCommuteOwner(commuteId: string): ResourceOwner {\n  return { ownerId: commuteId, isPublic: false };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Authorization Middleware\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Verify that the authenticated user has access to a specific resource.\n * Used for operations on specific user-owned resources (trips, subscriptions, etc.).\n */\nexport function requireResourceAccess(\n  resourceType: ResourceType,\n  action: PermissionAction,\n  options: {\n    /** Admin scope bypasses ownership checks */\n    adminBypass?: boolean;\n    /** Custom access check function */\n    customCheck?: (context: Context, auth: AuthContext) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;\n    /** Resource ID parameter name (for extracting from route params) */\n    resourceIdParam?: string;\n  } = {}\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  const { adminBypass = true, customCheck } = options;\n\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    // Must be authenticated\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Admin bypass check\n    if (adminBypass && auth.scope === \"admin\") {\n      logger.info(\"Admin bypass for resource access\", {\n        keyId: auth.keyId,\n        resourceType,\n        action,\n      });\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Scope-based authorization check\n    const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n    const requiredScopeForAction: Record<PermissionAction, ApiKeyScope> = {\n      create: \"write\",\n      read: \"read\",\n      update: \"write\",\n      delete: \"write\",\n      admin: \"admin\",\n    };\n\n    const requiredScope = requiredScopeForAction[action];\n    if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < scopeLevels[requiredScope]) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: `Insufficient permissions. Required scope: ${requiredScope}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Custom access check if provided\n    if (customCheck) {\n      const hasAccess = await customCheck(c, auth);\n      if (!hasAccess) {\n        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: \"Access denied to this resource\",\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require admin scope for privileged operations.\n * Use for operations that affect system configuration or other users.\n */\nexport function requireAdmin(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin\", \"admin\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    if (auth.scope !== \"admin\") {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin\", \"admin\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Admin privileges required\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"Admin operation authorized\", { keyId: auth.keyId });\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require write scope for state-changing operations.\n * Use as a simpler alternative to requireResourceAccess for general write operations.\n */\nexport function requireWrite(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"api\", \"write\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n    if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < 2) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"api\", \"write\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Write permissions required\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Optional authorization - attaches auth context if present but doesn't require it.\n * Use for endpoints that have enhanced features for authenticated users.\n *\n * Note: This is already provided by authentication.ts but re-exported for convenience.\n */\nexport { optionalAuth, isAuthenticated } from \"./authentication.js\";\n\n/**\n * Rate limit tier enforcement.\n * Ensures API key rate limits are respected based on assigned tier.\n */\nexport function enforceRateLimitTier(maxTier?: number): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      // Unauthenticated requests use default rate limiting (middleware level)\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Check if the API key's rate limit tier is within bounds\n    if (maxTier !== undefined && auth.rateLimitTier > maxTier) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rate_limit\", \"tier_exceeded\");\n      throw new HTTPException(429, {\n        message: \"Rate limit tier exceeded for this operation\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Cross-Origin Protection for State Changes\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Verify same-origin for state-changing operations.\n * Prevents CSRF attacks by validating the Origin header for POST/DELETE/PATCH.\n */\nexport function requireSameOrigin(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const method = c.req.method;\n\n    // Only apply to state-changing methods\n    if (![\"POST\", \"DELETE\", \"PATCH\", \"PUT\"].includes(method)) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    const origin = c.req.header(\"Origin\");\n    const host = c.req.header(\"Host\");\n    const referer = c.req.header(\"Referer\");\n\n    // Skip for API calls with proper authentication (API key or session)\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n    if (auth && (auth.authMethod === \"api_key\" || auth.authMethod === \"session\")) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // For browser-based requests without auth, check Origin/Referer\n    if (!origin && !referer) {\n      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"missing_origin_referer\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Origin or Referer header required for state-changing operations\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Validate Origin matches Host (if Origin is present)\n    if (origin && host) {\n      const originHost = new URL(origin).host;\n      if (originHost !== host) {\n        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"origin_mismatch\");\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: \"Cross-origin requests not allowed for state-changing operations\",\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Validate Referer matches Host (if Referer is present and no Origin)\n    if (referer && host && !origin) {\n      const refererHost = new URL(referer).host;\n      if (refererHost !== host) {\n        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"referer_mismatch\");\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: \"Cross-origin requests not allowed for state-changing operations\",\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Data Access Validation\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Validate that a request only accesses data the user is allowed to see.\n * Used for queries that might return user-scoped data.\n */\nexport function validateDataAccess(resourceType: ResourceType): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    // For authenticated users, ensure they can only access their own data\n    if (auth) {\n      // Check query parameters for user-scoped filters\n      const userId = c.req.query(\"userId\");\n      const endpoint = c.req.query(\"endpoint\");\n\n      // If trying to access another user's data, require admin scope\n      if ((userId || endpoint) && auth.scope !== \"admin\") {\n        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, \"read\");\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: \"Access to other users' data requires admin privileges\",\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Authorization Check Utilities\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Check if the current context has authorization for a specific action.\n * Returns an AuthorizationResult with details.\n */\nexport function checkAuthorization(\n  c: Context,\n  resourceType: ResourceType,\n  action: PermissionAction\n): AuthorizationResult {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  if (!auth) {\n    return {\n      allowed: false,\n      reason: \"Not authenticated\",\n      required: \"Authentication required\",\n    };\n  }\n\n  const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n  const requiredScopeForAction: Record<PermissionAction, ApiKeyScope> = {\n    create: \"write\",\n    read: \"read\",\n    update: \"write\",\n    delete: \"write\",\n    admin: \"admin\",\n  };\n\n  const requiredScope = requiredScopeForAction[action];\n  if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < scopeLevels[requiredScope]) {\n    return {\n      allowed: false,\n      reason: \"Insufficient permissions\",\n      required: requiredScope,\n    };\n  }\n\n  return { allowed: true };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require MFA verification for sensitive operations.\n * Use for operations that should require multi-factor authentication.\n */\nexport function requireMfa(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"mfa\", \"required\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Check if MFA was verified for this session\n    if (!auth.mfaVerified) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"mfa\", \"required\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Multi-factor authentication required for this operation\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"MFA-verified operation authorized\", { keyId: auth.keyId });\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Audit log wrapper for privileged operations.\n * Logs all access to sensitive resources for compliance.\n */\nexport function auditLogAccess(\n  resourceType: ResourceType,\n  action: PermissionAction\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n    const clientIp =\n      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n      \"unknown\";\n\n    // Log the attempt\n    logger.info(\"Privileged operation attempt\", {\n      keyId: auth?.keyId,\n      resourceType,\n      action,\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n      clientIp,\n      authenticated: !!auth,\n    });\n\n    // Execute the operation\n    await next();\n\n    // Log the result\n    const status = c.res.status;\n    const success = status >= 200 && status < 300;\n\n    logger.info(\"Privileged operation completed\", {\n      keyId: auth?.keyId,\n      resourceType,\n      action,\n      path: c.req.path,\n      status,\n      success,\n      clientIp,\n    });\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Scope Restriction Helpers\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Get the owner ID for an authenticated user, or \"anonymous\" for unauthenticated.\n * This is used to scope database queries to the user's own data.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param allowAdminAll - If true, admins get undefined (no filter), otherwise they also get their own keyId\n * @returns Owner ID to use in queries, or undefined for admins to see all data\n */\nexport function getOwnerIdForAuthContext(\n  c: Context,\n  allowAdminAll: boolean = true\n): string | undefined {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Unauthenticated users get anonymous\n  if (!auth) {\n    return \"anonymous\";\n  }\n\n  // Admins can see all data if allowAdminAll is true\n  if (allowAdminAll && auth.role === \"admin\") {\n    return undefined; // No filter - return all data\n  }\n\n  // Regular authenticated users get their own keyId\n  return auth.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply ownership scope to query parameters.\n * Ensures non-admin users can only query their own resources.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param params - Existing query parameters\n * @returns Parameters with ownership filter applied\n */\nexport function applyOwnershipScope<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  c: Context,\n  params: T\n): T & { ownerId?: string } {\n  const ownerId = getOwnerIdForAuthContext(c, true);\n\n  if (ownerId === undefined) {\n    // Admin user - no ownership filter\n    return params as T & { ownerId?: string };\n  }\n\n  // Non-admin user - add ownership filter\n  return { ...params, ownerId };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require ownership of a resource for access.\n * This is a helper that validates ownership before allowing access.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param resourceOwnerId - Owner ID of the resource being accessed\n * @returns true if access is allowed, throws HTTPException otherwise\n */\nexport function requireResourceOwnership(c: Context, resourceOwnerId: string): boolean {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Admins bypass ownership checks\n  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Check if the user owns the resource\n  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n\n  if (userOwnerId !== resourceOwnerId && resourceOwnerId !== \"anonymous\") {\n    securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"resource\", \"ownership\");\n    throw new HTTPException(403, {\n      message: \"Access denied: you do not own this resource\",\n    });\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Scope options for database queries.\n */\nexport interface ScopeOptions {\n  /** Owner ID to filter by (undefined = no filter, return all) */\n  ownerId?: string;\n  /** Additional filter criteria */\n  filters?: Record<string, unknown>;\n  /** Limit on number of results */\n  limit?: number;\n  /** Offset for pagination */\n  offset?: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create scoped query options for database operations.\n * Automatically applies ownership filtering based on authentication.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param baseOptions - Base query options\n * @returns Scoped options with ownership filter applied\n */\nexport function createScopedQuery<T extends ScopeOptions>(c: Context, baseOptions: T = {} as T): T {\n  const ownerId = getOwnerIdForAuthContext(c, true);\n\n  if (ownerId === undefined) {\n    // Admin - return all data without ownership filter\n    return baseOptions;\n  }\n\n  // Non-admin - apply ownership filter\n  return {\n    ...baseOptions,\n    ownerId,\n  } as T;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if the current user can access another user's data.\n * Returns true if the user is an admin or if the targetUserId matches their own.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param targetUserId - The user ID being accessed\n * @returns true if access is allowed\n */\nexport function canAccessUserData(c: Context, targetUserId: string): boolean {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Admins can access any user's data\n  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Users can only access their own data\n  return auth?.keyId === targetUserId;\n}\n\n/**\n * Filter a response to include only user-scoped data.\n * Removes sensitive information and ensures users only see their own data.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param data - Data to filter\n * @param ownerField - Field name that contains the owner ID\n * @returns Filtered data\n */\nexport function filterUserScopedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  c: Context,\n  data: T[],\n  ownerField: string = \"ownerId\"\n): T[] {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Admins see all data\n  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n    return data;\n  }\n\n  // Non-admins only see their own data\n  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n\n  return data.filter((item) => {\n    const ownerId = item[ownerField] as string | undefined;\n    return ownerId === userOwnerId || ownerId === \"anonymous\";\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate that a list request is properly scoped.\n * Throws an error if a non-admin user tries to request another user's data.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param requestedOwnerId - Owner ID from request (if any)\n * @throws HTTPException if access is denied\n */\nexport function validateListScope(c: Context, requestedOwnerId?: string): void {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // If no specific owner requested, this is fine (will be filtered by applyOwnershipScope)\n  if (!requestedOwnerId) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Admins can request any owner's data\n  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Non-admins can only request their own data\n  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n\n  if (requestedOwnerId !== userOwnerId) {\n    securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"list\", \"scope\");\n    throw new HTTPException(403, {\n      message: \"Access denied: you can only view your own data\",\n    });\n  }\n}\n","numLines":680,"startLine":1,"totalLines":680}}}

============================================================================\n147\t// IP Address Utilities\n148\t// ============================================================================\n149\t\n150\t/**\n151\t * Parse an IP address into its components.\n152\t */\n153\texport function parseIpAddress(ip: IPv6\n169\t    const hextets = ip.split(\":\");\n170\t\n171\t    return {\n172\t      ip,\n173\t      type: \"ipv6\",\n174\t      hextets,\n175\t      isPrivate: ip.startsWith(\"fc\") || [first, second] = octets;\n193\t\n194\t  // 10.0.0.0/8\n195\t  if (first === 10) return true;\n196\t\n197\t  // 172.16.0.0/12\n198\t  if (first === 172 && second >= 16 && second <= 31) return true;\n199\t\n200\t  // 192.168.0.0/16\n201\t  if (first === 192 && second === 168) return true;\n202\t\n203\t  // 169.254.0.0/16 (link-local)\n204\t  if (first === 169 && second === 254) return true;\n205\t\n206\t  return false;\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\t/**\n210\t * Check if two IP addresses are in the same subnet.\n211\t *\n212\t * For IPv4, checks the specified number of leading octets.\n213\t * For IPv6, checks the specified number of leading hextets.\n214\t */\n215\texport function areIpsInSameSubnet(ip1: string, ip2: string, prefixLength = 24): boolean {\n216\t  const info1 = parseIpAddress(ip1);\n217\t  const info2 = parseIpAddress(ip2);\n218\t\n219\t  if (info1.type !== info2.type || info1.type === \"unknown\") {\n220\t    return false;\n221\t  }\n222\t\n223\t  if (info1.type === \"ipv4\" && info1.octets && info2.octets) {\n224\t    // For IPv4, prefixLength / 8 gives number of octets to compare\n225\t    const octetsToCompare = Math.floor(prefixLength / 8);\n226\t\n227\t    for (let i = 0; i < octetsToCompare && i < 4; i++) {\n228\t      if (info1.octets[i] !== info2.octets[i]) {\n229\t        return false;\n230\t      }\n231\t    }\n232\t\n233\t    // Check remaining bits\n234\t    const remainingBits = prefixLength % 8;\n235\t    if (remainingBits > 0 && octetsToCompare < 4) {\n236\t      const mask = 256 - Math.pow(2, 8 - remainingBits);\n237\t      if ((info1.octets[octetsToCompare]! & mask) !== (info2.octets[octetsToCompare]! & mask)) {\n238\t        return false;\n239\t      }\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    return true;\n243\t  }\n244\t\n245\t  if (info1.type === \"ipv6\" && info1.hextets && info2.hextets) {\n246\t    // For IPv6, prefixLength / 16 gives number of hextets to compare\n247\t    const hextetsToCompare = Math.floor(prefixLength / 16);\n248\t\n249\t    for (let i = 0; i < hextetsToCompare && i < 8; i++) {\n250\t      if (info1.hextets[i] !== info2.hextets[i]) {\n251\t        return false;\n252\t      }\n253\t    }\n254\t\n255\t    return true;\n256\t  }\n257\t\n258\t  return false;\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\t/**\n262\t * Calculate the distance between two IP addresses.\n263\t *\n264\t * Returns a normalized \"distance\" value (0-100) based on how different the IPs are.\n265\t * Useful for detecting proxy/VPN changes vs. location changes.\n266\t */\n267\texport function calculateIpDistance(ip1: string, ip2: string): number {\n268\t  const info1 = parseIpAddress(ip1);\n269\t  const info2 = parseIpAddress(ip2);\n270\t\n271\t  if (info1.type !== info2.type || info1.type === \"unknown\") {\n272\t    return 100; // Maximum distance for different IP types\n273\t  }\n274\t\n275\t  if (info1.type === \"ipv4\" && info1.octets && info2.octets) {\n276\t    // Calculate difference for each octet\n277\t    let totalDiff = 0;\n278\t    for (let i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n279\t      totalDiff += Math.abs(info1.octets[i]! - info2.octets[i]!);\n280\t    }\n281\t\n282\t    // Normalize to 0-100\n283\t    return Math.min(100, Math.floor(totalDiff / 2.55));\n284\t  }\n285\t\n286\t  return 50; // Middle distance for IPv6 (simplified)\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\t/**\n290\t * Get a simplified IP class for grouping.\n291\t *\n292\t * Returns \"A\", \"B\", \"C\" class for IPv4, useful for coarse-grained comparison.\n293\t */\n294\texport function getIpClass(ip: string): string {\n295\t  const info = parseIpAddress(ip);\n296\t\n297\t  if (info.type === \"ipv4\" && info.octets) {\n298\t    const [first] = info.octets;\n299\t\n300\t    if (first < 128) return \"A\";\n301\t    if (first < 192) return \"B\";\n302\t    return \"C\";\n303\t  }\n304\t\n305\t  return \"unknown\";\n306\t}\n307\t\n308\t// ============================================================================\n309\t// User Agent Analysis\n310\t// ============================================================================\n311\t\n312\t/**\n313\t * Analyze a user agent string.\n314\t */\n315\texport function analyzeUserAgent(userAgent: string): = \"opera\";\n327\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"brave/\")) {\n328\t    info.browser = \"brave\";\n329\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"chrome/\") && !ua.includes(\"edg\")) {\n330\t    info.browser = \"chrome\";\n331\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"safari/\") && !ua.includes(\"chrome\")) {\n332\t    info.browser = \"safari\";\n333\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"firefox/\")) {\n334\t    info.browser = \"firefox\";\n335\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"trident/\") || ua.includes(\"msie\")) {\n336\t    info.browser = \"ie\";\n337\t  }\n338\t\n339\t  // Detect OS\n340\t  if (ua.includes(\"windows\")) {\n341\t    info.os = \"windows\";\n342\t    if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 10.0\")) info.osVersion = \"10\";\n343\t    else if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 6.3\")) info.osVersion = \"8.1\";\n344\t    else if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 6.1\")) info.osVersion = \"7\";\n345\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"iphone\") || ua.includes(\"ipad\") || ua.includes(\"ipod\")) {\n346\t    info.os = \"ios\";\n347\t  } else (ua.includes(\"linux\")) {\n352\t    info.os = \"linux\";\n353\t  }\n354\t\n355\t  // Detect device type\n356\t  if (/bot|crawl|spider|slurp|curl|wget/i.test(userAgent)) {\n357\t    info.deviceType = \"bot\";\n358\t  } else if (/tablet|ipad|playbook|silk/i.test(ua)) {\n359\t    info.deviceType = \"tablet\";\n360\t  } else if (/mobile|android|iphone|ipod|blackberry|iemobile|opera mini/i.test(ua))   info.hardware = \"arm\";\n369\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"x86_64\") || ua.includes(\"x64\") || ua.includes(\"wow64\")) {\n370\t    info.hardware = \"x64\";\n371\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"i686\") || ua.includes(\"x86\")) {\n372\t    info.hardware = \"x86\";\n373\t  }\n374\t\n375\t  return info;\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t/**\n379\t * Calculate similarity between two user agent strings.\n380\t *\n381\t * Returns 0-100 similarity score.\n382\t */\n383\texport function calculateUserAgentSimilarity(ua1: string, ua2: string): number {\n384\t  const info1 = analyzeUserAgent(ua1);\n385\t  const info2 = analyzeUserAgent(ua2);\n386\t\n387\t  let similarity = 0;\n388\t  let factors = 0;\n389\t\n390\t  // Browser match\n391\t  if (info1.browser === info2.browser) {\n392\t    similarity += 30;\n393\t  }\n394\t  factors++;\n395\t\n396\t  // OS match\n397\t  if (info1.os === info2.os) {\n398\t    similarity += 30;\n399\t  }\n400\t  factors++;\n401\t\n402\t  // Device type match\n403\t  if (info1.deviceType === info2.deviceType) {\n404\t    similarity += 20;\n405\t  }\n406\t  factors++;\n407\t\n408\t  // Hardware match\n409\t  if (info1.hardware === info2.hardware) {\n410\t    similarity += 20;\n411\t  }\n412\t  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n413\t  factors++;\n414\t\n415\t  return Math.min(100, similarity);\n416\t}\n417\t\n418\t/**\n419\t * Detect if user agent change is legitimate.\n420\t *\n421\t * Returns true if the change is likely a browser update or minor version change.\n422\t */\n423\texport function isLegitimateUserAgentChange(oldUa: string, newUa: string): boolean {\n424\t  const oldInfo = analyzeUserAgent(oldUa);\n425\t  const newInfo = analyzeUserAgent(newUa);\n426\t\n427\t  // Same browser and OS is likely a version update\n428\t  if (oldInfo.browser === newInfo.browser && oldInfo.os === newInfo.os) {\n429\t    return true;\n430\t  }\n431\t\n432\t  // Same device type with different browser could be user trying new browser\n433\t  if (oldInfo.deviceType === newInfo.deviceType && oldInfo.os === newInfo.os) {\n434\t    return true;\n435\t  }\n436\t\n437\t  return false;\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\t// ============================================================================\n441\t// Session Risk Assessment\n442\t// ============================================================================\n443\t\n444\t/**\n445\t * Security event tracking for session risk scoring.\n446\t */\n447\tconst sessionSecurityEvents = new Map<string, SecurityEvent[]>();\n448\t\n449\t/**\n450\t * Assess session security risk.\n451\t *\n452\t * Analyzes a session and current request to calculate a risk score.\n453\t */\n454\texport async function assessSessionRisk(\n455\t  session: AuthSession,\n456\t  currentIp: string,\n457\t  currentUserAgent?: string,\n458\t  options: {\n459\t    /** Time window for event history (ms) knownGeo?: (ip: string) => Promise<GeolocationData | null>;\n465\t  } = {}\n466\t): Promise<SessionRiskAssessment> {\n467\t  const {\n468\t    eventHistoryWindow = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 24 hours\n469\t    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n470\t    includeGeolocation = false,\n471\t    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n472\t    knownGeo,\n473\t  } = options;\n474\t\n475\t  const riskFactors: string[] = [];\n476\t  const events: SecurityEvent[] = [];\n477\t  let riskScore = 0;\n478\t\n479\t  const now = Date.now();\n480\t\n481\t  // Get recent security events for this session\n482\t  const recentEvents = (sessionSecurityEvents.get(session.sessionId) || []).filter(\n483\t    (e) => now - e.timestamp < eventHistoryWindow\n484\t  );\n485\t\n486\t  // Check IP change\n487\t  if (session.clientIp !== currentIp) {\n488\t    const ipInfo = parseIpAddress(currentIp);\n489\t    const oldIpInfo = parseIpAddress(session.clientIp);\n490\t\n491\t    if (ipInfo.type !== oldIpInfo.type) {\n492\t      riskScore += 40;\n493\t      riskFactors.push(\"IP address type changed (IPv4 ↔ IPv6)\");\n494\t      events.push({\n495\t        type: \"ip_type_change\",\n496\t        timestamp: now,\n497\t        score: 40,\n498\t        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n499\t      });\n500\t    } else if (!areIpsInSameSubnet(session.clientIp, currentIp, 24)) {\n501\t      riskScore += 30;\n502\t      riskFactors.push(\"IP address changed to different subnet\");\n503\t      events.push({\n504\t        type: \"ip_subnet_change\",\n505\t        timestamp: now,\n506\t        score: 30,\n507\t        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n508\t      });\n509\t    } else {\n510\t      riskScore += 10;\n511\t      riskFactors.push(\"IP address changed within same subnet\");\n512\t      events.push({\n513\t        type: \"ip_change_same_subnet\",\n514\t        timestamp: now,\n515\t        score: 10,\n516\t        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n517\t      });\n518\t    }\n519\t  }\n520\t\n521\t  // Check User-Agent change\n522\t  if (currentUserAgent && session.userAgent && session.userAgent !== currentUserAgent) {\n523\t    const similarity = calculateUserAgentSimilarity(session.userAgent, currentUserAgent);\n524\t\n525\t    if (similarity < 50) {\n526\t      riskScore += 30;\n527\t      riskFactors.push(\"User-Agent changed significantly\");\n528\t      events.push({\n529\t        type: \"ua_change_major\",\n530\t        timestamp: now,\n531\t        score: 30,\n532\t        details: { oldUa: session.userAgent, newUa: currentUserAgent, similarity },\n533\t      });\n534\t    } else if (!isLegitimateUserAgentChange(session.userAgent, currentUserAgent)) {\n535\t      riskScore += 15;\n536\t      riskFactors.push(\"User-Agent changed (possibly suspicious)\");\n537\t      events.push({\n538\t        type: \"ua_change_minor\",\n539\t        timestamp: now,\n540\t        score: 15,\n541\t        details: { oldUa: session.userAgent, newUa: currentUserAgent },\n542\t      });\n543\t    }\n544\t  }\n545\t\n546\t  // Check session age (very new sessions are higher risk)\n547\t  const sessionAge = now - session.createdAt;\n548\t  if (sessionAge < 5 * 60 * 1000) {\n549\t    // Less than 5 minutes old\n550\t    riskScore += 5;\n551\t    riskFactors.push(\"Very new session\");\n552\t  }\n553\t\n554\t  // Check idle time (long idle may indicate hijacking)\n555\t  const idleTime = now - session.lastActivityAt;\n556\t  if (idleTime > 60 * 60 * 1000) {\n557\t    // More than 1 hour idle\n558\t    riskScore += 10;\n559\t    riskFactors.push(\"Long idle time before activity\");\n560\t  }\n561\t\n562\t  // Include recent security events\n563\t  for (const event of recentEvents) {\n564\t    riskScore += event.score;\n565\t    events.push(event);\n566\t  }\n567\t\n568\t  // Cap risk score at 100\n569\t  riskScore = Math.min(100, riskScore);\n570\t\n571\t  // Determine risk level\n572\t  let riskLevel: \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n573\t  let recommendedAction: \"allow\" | \"monitor\" | \"challenge\" | \"block\";\n574\t\n575\t  if (riskScore < 20) {\n576\t    riskLevel = \"low\";\n577\t    recommendedAction = \"allow\";\n578\t  } else if (riskScore < 50) {\n579\t    riskLevel = \"medium\";\n580\t    recommendedAction = \"monitor\";\n581\t  } else if (riskScore < 80) {\n582\t    riskLevel = \"high\";\n583\t    recommendedAction = \"challenge\";\n584\t  } else {\n585\t    riskLevel = \"critical\";\n586\t    recommendedAction = \"block\";\n587\t  }\n588\t\n589\t  return {\n590\t    riskScore,\n591\t    riskLevel,\n592\t    riskFactors,\n593\t    recommendedAction,\n594\t    events,\n595\t  };\n596\t}\n597\t\n598\t/**\n599\t * Record a security event for a session.\n600\t */\n601\texport function recordSecurityEvent(sessionId: string, event: SecurityEvent): void {\n602\t  const events = sessionSecurityEvents.get(sessionId) || [];\n603\t  events.push(event);\n604\t\n605\t  // Keep only last 100 events per session\n606\t  if (events.length > 100) {\n607\t    events.shift();\n608\t  }\n609\t\n610\t  sessionSecurityEvents.set(sessionId, events);\n611\t\n612\t  logger.debug(\"Security event recorded\", {\n613\t    sessionId,\n614\t    eventType: event.type,\n615\t    score: event.score,\n616\t  });\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t/**\n620\t * Clear security events for a session.\n621\t */\n622\texport function clearSecurityEvents(sessionId: string): void {\n623\t  sessionSecurityEvents.delete(sessionId);\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// ============================================================================\n627\t// Device Trust Management\n628\t// DeviceTrustInfo>();\n634\t\n635\t/**\n636\t * Get or create device trust info.\n637\t */\n638\texport function getOrCreateDeviceTrust(\n639\t  deviceId: string,\n640\t  initialRiskAssessment?: lastRiskAssessment: initialRiskAssessment,\n655\t  };\n656\t\n657\t  deviceTrustStorage.set(deviceId, trustInfo);\n658\t\n659\t  logger.info(\"New device trust record created\", { deviceId firstSeenAt\n671\t  const now = Date.now();\n672\t  trustInfo.lastSeenAt = Math.max(now, trustInfo.firstSeenAt + 1);\n673\t  trustInfo.successfulAuths++;\n674\t  trustInfo.lastRiskAssessment = riskAssessment;\n675\t\n676\t  // Auto-promote trust based on successful auths\n677\t  if (trustInfo.successfulAuths >= 10 && trustInfo.trustLevel !== \"highly_trusted\") {\n678\t \"unknown\") {\n681\t    trustInfo.trustLevel = \"trusted\";\n682\t    logger.info(\"Device promoted to trusted\", { deviceId });\n683\t  } else if (riskAssessment && riskAssessment.riskLevel === \"high\") {\n684\t    trustInfo.trustLevel = \"untrusted\";\n685\t    logger.warn(\"Device marked as untrusted due to high risk\", { deviceId });\n686\t  }\n687\t\n688\t ============================================================================\n726\t// Impossible Travel Detection\n727\t// ============================================================================\n728\t\n729\t/**\n730\t * Maximum plausible travel speed (km/h).\n731\t * Commercial jets fly at ~900 km/h, so anything faster is suspicious.\n732\t */\n733\tconst MAX_PLAUSIBLE_SPEED_KMH = 900;\n734\t\n735\t/**\n736\t * Check for impossible travel between two locations.\n737\t *\n738\t * Compares the time between two events with the distance between\n739\t * their locations to detect impossible travel scenarios.\n740\t */\n741\texport async function detectImpossibleTravel(\n742\t  event1: { timestamp: number; ip: string; geo?: GeolocationData },\n743\t  event2: { timestamp: number; ip: string; geo?: GeolocationData },\n744\t  options: {\n745\t    /** Maximum plausible speed (km/h) */\n746\t    maxSpeedKmh?: number;\n747\t    /** Geolocation lookup function */\n748\t    geoLookup?: (ip: string) => Promise<GeolocationData | null>;\n749\t  } = {}\n750\t): Promise<{ impossible: boolean; speed?: number; distance?: number }> {\n751\t  const { maxSpeedKmh = MAX_PLAUSIBLE_SPEED_KMH, geoLookup } = options;\n752\t\n753\t  // Ensure event1 is earlier\n754\t  const [earlier, later] =\n755\t    event1.timestamp < event2.timestamp ? [event1, event2] : [event2, event1];\n756\t\n757\t  // Get geolocation data\n758\t  const geo1 = event1.geo || (geoLookup ? await geoLookup(event1.ip) : null);\n759\t  const geo2 = event2.geo || (geoLookup ? await geoLookup(event2.ip) : null);\n760\t\n761\t  if (!geo1 || !geo2 || !geo1.latitude || !geo1.longitude || !geo2.latitude || !geo2.longitude) {\n762\t    // Can't determine without geolocation\n763\t    return { impossible: false };\n764\t  }\n765\t\n766\t  // Calculate distance using Haversine formula\n767\t  const distance = calculateDistance(geo1.latitude, geo1.longitude, geo2.latitude, geo2.longitude);\n768\t\n769\t  // Calculate time difference in hours\n770\t  const timeDiffHours = (later.timestamp - earlier.timestamp) / (1000 * 60 * 60);\n771\t\n772\t  if (timeDiffHours <= 0) {\n773\t    // Events at the same time or reversed timestamps\n774\t    return { impossible: false };\n775\t  }\n776\t\n777\t  // Calculate required speed\n778\t  const speed = distance / timeDiffHours;\n779\t\n780\t  if (speed > maxSpeedKmh) {\n781\t    return { impossible: true, speed, distance };\n782\t  }\n783\t\n784\t  return { impossible: false, speed, distance };\n785\t}\n786\t\n787\t/**\n788\t * Calculate distance between two coordinates using the Haversine formula.\n789\t *\n790\t * Returns distance in kilometers.\n791\t */\n792\texport function calculateDistance(lat1: number, lon1: number, lat2: number, lon2: number): number {\n793\t  const R = 6371; // Earth's radius in km\n794\t  const dLat = toRadians(lat2 - lat1);\n795\t  const dLon = toRadians(lon2 - lon1);\n796\t\n797\t  const a =\n798\t    Math.sin(dLat / 2) * Math.sin(dLat / 2) +\n799\t    Math.cos(toRadians(lat1)) * Math.cos(toRadians(lat2)) * Math.sin(dLon / 2) * Math.sin(dLon / 2);\n800\t\n801\t  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a));\n802\t\n803\t  return R * c;\n804\t}\n805\t\n806\t/**\n807\t * Convert degrees to radians.\n808\t */\n809\tfunction toRadians(degrees: number): number {\n810\t  return degrees * (Math.PI / 180);\n811\t}\n812\t\n813\t// ============================================================================\n814\t// Middleware Integration\n815\t// ============================================================================\n816\t\n817\t/**\n818\t * Session security middleware options.\n819\t */\n820\texport interface
