function sessionSecurity(options: SessionSecurityMiddlewareOptions = {}) {\n837\t  const {\n838\t    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n839\t    enforceIpBinding = true,\n840\t    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n841\t    checkUserAgent = true,\n842\t    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n843\t    riskThreshold = 80,\n844\t    reauthOnHighRisk = true,\n845\t  } = options;\n846\t\n847\t  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any\n848\t  return async (c: any, next: any) => {\n849\t    const session = c.get(\"session\");\n850\t    if (!session) {\n851\t      return next();\n852\t    }\n853\t\n854\t    const clientIp =\n855\t      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n856\t      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n857\t      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n858\t      \"unknown\";\n859\t\n860\t    const userAgent = c.req.header(\"User-Agent\");\n861\t\n862\t    // Assess session risk\n863\t    const riskAssessment = await assessSessionRisk(session, clientIp, userAgent);\n864\t\n865\t    // Attach risk assessment to context\n866\t    c.set(\"sessionRiskAssessment\", riskAssessment);\n867\t\n868\t    // Take action based on risk level\n869\t    if (riskAssessment.recommendedAction === \"block\") {\n870\t      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n871\t        c,\n872\t        \"high_risk_session_blocked\",\n873\t        `Session blocked due to high risk score: ${riskAssessment.riskScore}`\n874\t      );\n875\t      throw new Error(\"Session blocked due to suspicious activity\");\n876\t    }\n877\t\n878\t    if (riskAssessment.recommendedAction === \"challenge\" && reauthOnHighRisk) {\n879\t      // Require re-authentication for high-risk sessions\n880\t      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n881\t        c,\n882\t        \"high_risk_session_challenge\",\n883\t        `Re-authentication required due to risk score: ${riskAssessment.riskScore}`\n884\t      );\n885\t      throw new Error(\"Re-authentication required\");\n886\t    }\n887\t\n888\t    return next();\n889\t  };\n890\t}\n891\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c93cc6db-9604-447b-bedb-ccf0fc966d30","uuid":"fa2a575a-5a06-47bd-9b5c-a53fdc94cf30","timestamp":"2026-05-09T04:04:40.932Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/packages/server/src/middleware/session-security.ts","content":"/**\n * Enhanced session security utilities.\n *\n * Provides advanced security features for session management including:\n * - IP address validation and subnet matching\n * - User agent analysis and fingerprinting\n * - Geolocation-based security checks\n * - Impossible travel detection\n * - Session risk scoring\n * - Device trust management\n *\n * These utilities complement the core authentication system with\n * additional security layers for detecting and preventing session hijacking.\n */\n\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport type { AuthSession } from \"./authentication.js\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Types and Interfaces\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * IP address type.\n */\nexport type IpType = \"ipv4\" | \"ipv6\" | \"unknown\";\n\n/**\n * IP address information.\n */\nexport interface IpInfo {\n  /** The IP address */\n  ip: string;\n  /** IP type (IPv4 or IPv6) */\n  type: IpType;\n  /** IPv4 octets (for IPv4 addresses) */\n  octets?: number[];\n  /** IPv6 hextets (for IPv6 addresses) */\n  hextets?: string[];\n  /** Whether this is a private/local IP */\n  isPrivate: boolean;\n  /** Whether this is a loopback IP */\n  isLoopback: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * IP subnet definition.\n */\nexport interface IpSubnet {\n  /** IP address */\n  ip: string;\n  /** CIDR prefix length (e.g., 24 for /24) */\n  prefixLength: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * User agent analysis result.\n */\nexport interface UserAgentInfo {\n  /** Full user agent string */\n  raw: string;\n  /** Browser family */\n  browser?: string;\n  /** Browser version (if detectable) */\n  browserVersion?: string;\n  /** OS family */\n  os?: string;\n  /** OS version (if detectable) */\n  osVersion?: string;\n  /** Device type */\n  deviceType: \"mobile\" | \"tablet\" | \"desktop\" | \"bot\" | \"unknown\";\n  /** Device hardware info (if available) */\n  hardware?: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Geolocation data (simplified).\n */\nexport interface GeolocationData {\n  /** Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) */\n  country?: string;\n  /** City name */\n  city?: string;\n  /** Latitude */\n  latitude?: number;\n  /** Longitude */\n  longitude?: number;\n  /** Approximate timezone */\n  timezone?: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Security event for risk scoring.\n */\nexport interface SecurityEvent {\n  /** Event type */\n  type: string;\n  /** Event timestamp */\n  timestamp: number;\n  /** Risk score contribution */\n  score: number;\n  /** Event details */\n  details?: Record<string, unknown>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Session risk assessment.\n */\nexport interface SessionRiskAssessment {\n  /** Overall risk score (0-100) */\n  riskScore: number;\n  /** Risk level */\n  riskLevel: \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n  /** Risk factors identified */\n  riskFactors: string[];\n  /** Recommended action */\n  recommendedAction: \"allow\" | \"monitor\" | \"challenge\" | \"block\";\n  /** Security events that contributed to the score */\n  events: SecurityEvent[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Device trust level.\n */\nexport type DeviceTrustLevel = \"unknown\" | \"untrusted\" | \"trusted\" | \"highly_trusted\";\n\n/**\n * Device trust information.\n */\nexport interface DeviceTrustInfo {\n  /** Device ID */\n  deviceId: string;\n  /** Trust level */\n  trustLevel: DeviceTrustLevel;\n  /** First seen timestamp */\n  firstSeenAt: number;\n  /** Last seen timestamp */\n  lastSeenAt: number;\n  /** Number of successful authentications */\n  successfulAuths: number;\n  /** Last risk assessment */\n  lastRiskAssessment?: SessionRiskAssessment;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// IP Address Utilities\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Parse an IP address into its components.\n */\nexport function parseIpAddress(ip: string): IpInfo {\n  if (ip.includes(\".\")) {\n    // IPv4\n    const octets = ip.split(\".\").map((n) => parseInt(n, 10));\n\n    return {\n      ip,\n      type: \"ipv4\",\n      octets,\n      isPrivate: isPrivateIpv4(octets),\n      isLoopback: octets[0] === 127,\n    };\n  }\n\n  if (ip.includes(\":\")) {\n    // IPv6\n    const hextets = ip.split(\":\");\n\n    return {\n      ip,\n      type: \"ipv6\",\n      hextets,\n      isPrivate: ip.startsWith(\"fc\") || ip.startsWith(\"fd\") || ip.startsWith(\"fe80\"),\n      isLoopback: ip === \"::1\",\n    };\n  }\n\n  return {\n    ip,\n    type: \"unknown\",\n    isPrivate: false,\n    isLoopback: false,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if IPv4 address is private.\n */\nfunction isPrivateIpv4(octets: number[]): boolean {\n  const [first, second] = octets;\n\n  // 10.0.0.0/8\n  if (first === 10) return true;\n\n  // 172.16.0.0/12\n  if (first === 172 && second >= 16 && second <= 31) return true;\n\n  // 192.168.0.0/16\n  if (first === 192 && second === 168) return true;\n\n  // 169.254.0.0/16 (link-local)\n  if (first === 169 && second === 254) return true;\n\n  return false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if two IP addresses are in the same subnet.\n *\n * For IPv4, checks the specified number of leading octets.\n * For IPv6, checks the specified number of leading hextets.\n */\nexport function areIpsInSameSubnet(ip1: string, ip2: string, prefixLength = 24): boolean {\n  const info1 = parseIpAddress(ip1);\n  const info2 = parseIpAddress(ip2);\n\n  if (info1.type !== info2.type || info1.type === \"unknown\") {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  if (info1.type === \"ipv4\" && info1.octets && info2.octets) {\n    // For IPv4, prefixLength / 8 gives number of octets to compare\n    const octetsToCompare = Math.floor(prefixLength / 8);\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < octetsToCompare && i < 4; i++) {\n      if (info1.octets[i] !== info2.octets[i]) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check remaining bits\n    const remainingBits = prefixLength % 8;\n    if (remainingBits > 0 && octetsToCompare < 4) {\n      const mask = 256 - Math.pow(2, 8 - remainingBits);\n      if ((info1.octets[octetsToCompare]! & mask) !== (info2.octets[octetsToCompare]! & mask)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  if (info1.type === \"ipv6\" && info1.hextets && info2.hextets) {\n    // For IPv6, prefixLength / 16 gives number of hextets to compare\n    const hextetsToCompare = Math.floor(prefixLength / 16);\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < hextetsToCompare && i < 8; i++) {\n      if (info1.hextets[i] !== info2.hextets[i]) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate the distance between two IP addresses.\n *\n * Returns a normalized \"distance\" value (0-100) based on how different the IPs are.\n * Useful for detecting proxy/VPN changes vs. location changes.\n */\nexport function calculateIpDistance(ip1: string, ip2: string): number {\n  const info1 = parseIpAddress(ip1);\n  const info2 = parseIpAddress(ip2);\n\n  if (info1.type !== info2.type || info1.type === \"unknown\") {\n    return 100; // Maximum distance for different IP types\n  }\n\n  if (info1.type === \"ipv4\" && info1.octets && info2.octets) {\n    // Calculate difference for each octet\n    let totalDiff = 0;\n    for (let i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n      totalDiff += Math.abs(info1.octets[i]! - info2.octets[i]!);\n    }\n\n    // Normalize to 0-100\n    return Math.min(100, Math.floor(totalDiff / 2.55));\n  }\n\n  return 50; // Middle distance for IPv6 (simplified)\n}\n\n/**\n * Get a simplified IP class for grouping.\n *\n * Returns \"A\", \"B\", \"C\" class for IPv4, useful for coarse-grained comparison.\n */\nexport function getIpClass(ip: string): string {\n  const info = parseIpAddress(ip);\n\n  if (info.type === \"ipv4\" && info.octets) {\n    const [first] = info.octets;\n\n    if (first < 128) return \"A\";\n    if (first < 192) return \"B\";\n    return \"C\";\n  }\n\n  return \"unknown\";\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// User Agent Analysis\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Analyze a user agent string.\n */\nexport function analyzeUserAgent(userAgent: string): UserAgentInfo {\n  const ua = userAgent.toLowerCase();\n  const info: UserAgentInfo = {\n    raw: userAgent,\n    deviceType: \"unknown\",\n  };\n\n  // Detect browser\n  if (ua.includes(\"edg/\") || ua.includes(\"edge/\")) {\n    info.browser = \"edge\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"opr/\") || ua.includes(\"opera/\")) {\n    info.browser = \"opera\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"brave/\")) {\n    info.browser = \"brave\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"chrome/\") && !ua.includes(\"edg\")) {\n    info.browser = \"chrome\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"safari/\") && !ua.includes(\"chrome\")) {\n    info.browser = \"safari\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"firefox/\")) {\n    info.browser = \"firefox\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"trident/\") || ua.includes(\"msie\")) {\n    info.browser = \"ie\";\n  }\n\n  // Detect OS\n  if (ua.includes(\"windows\")) {\n    info.os = \"windows\";\n    if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 10.0\")) info.osVersion = \"10\";\n    else if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 6.3\")) info.osVersion = \"8.1\";\n    else if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 6.1\")) info.osVersion = \"7\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"iphone\") || ua.includes(\"ipad\") || ua.includes(\"ipod\")) {\n    info.os = \"ios\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"android\")) {\n    info.os = \"android\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"mac os x\") || ua.includes(\"macos\")) {\n    info.os = \"macos\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"linux\")) {\n    info.os = \"linux\";\n  }\n\n  // Detect device type\n  if (/bot|crawl|spider|slurp|curl|wget/i.test(userAgent)) {\n    info.deviceType = \"bot\";\n  } else if (/tablet|ipad|playbook|silk/i.test(ua)) {\n    info.deviceType = \"tablet\";\n  } else if (/mobile|android|iphone|ipod|blackberry|iemobile|opera mini/i.test(ua)) {\n    info.deviceType = \"mobile\";\n  } else if (info.os) {\n    info.deviceType = \"desktop\";\n  }\n\n  // Detect hardware\n  if (ua.includes(\"arm\")) {\n    info.hardware = \"arm\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"x86_64\") || ua.includes(\"x64\") || ua.includes(\"wow64\")) {\n    info.hardware = \"x64\";\n  } else if (ua.includes(\"i686\") || ua.includes(\"x86\")) {\n    info.hardware = \"x86\";\n  }\n\n  return info;\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate similarity between two user agent strings.\n *\n * Returns 0-100 similarity score.\n */\nexport function calculateUserAgentSimilarity(ua1: string, ua2: string): number {\n  const info1 = analyzeUserAgent(ua1);\n  const info2 = analyzeUserAgent(ua2);\n\n  let similarity = 0;\n  let factors = 0;\n\n  // Browser match\n  if (info1.browser === info2.browser) {\n    similarity += 30;\n  }\n  factors++;\n\n  // OS match\n  if (info1.os === info2.os) {\n    similarity += 30;\n  }\n  factors++;\n\n  // Device type match\n  if (info1.deviceType === info2.deviceType) {\n    similarity += 20;\n  }\n  factors++;\n\n  // Hardware match\n  if (info1.hardware === info2.hardware) {\n    similarity += 20;\n  }\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  factors++;\n\n  return Math.min(100, similarity);\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect if user agent change is legitimate.\n *\n * Returns true if the change is likely a browser update or minor version change.\n */\nexport function isLegitimateUserAgentChange(oldUa: string, newUa: string): boolean {\n  const oldInfo = analyzeUserAgent(oldUa);\n  const newInfo = analyzeUserAgent(newUa);\n\n  // Same browser and OS is likely a version update\n  if (oldInfo.browser === newInfo.browser && oldInfo.os === newInfo.os) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Same device type with different browser could be user trying new browser\n  if (oldInfo.deviceType === newInfo.deviceType && oldInfo.os === newInfo.os) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Session Risk Assessment\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Security event tracking for session risk scoring.\n */\nconst sessionSecurityEvents = new Map<string, SecurityEvent[]>();\n\n/**\n * Assess session security risk.\n *\n * Analyzes a session and current request to calculate a risk score.\n */\nexport async function assessSessionRisk(\n  session: AuthSession,\n  currentIp: string,\n  currentUserAgent?: string,\n  options: {\n    /** Time window for event history (ms) */\n    eventHistoryWindow?: number;\n    /** Whether to include geolocation checks */\n    includeGeolocation?: boolean;\n    /** Known geolocation data (optional) */\n    knownGeo?: (ip: string) => Promise<GeolocationData | null>;\n  } = {}\n): Promise<SessionRiskAssessment> {\n  const {\n    eventHistoryWindow = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 24 hours\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    includeGeolocation = false,\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    knownGeo,\n  } = options;\n\n  const riskFactors: string[] = [];\n  const events: SecurityEvent[] = [];\n  let riskScore = 0;\n\n  const now = Date.now();\n\n  // Get recent security events for this session\n  const recentEvents = (sessionSecurityEvents.get(session.sessionId) || []).filter(\n    (e) => now - e.timestamp < eventHistoryWindow\n  );\n\n  // Check IP change\n  if (session.clientIp !== currentIp) {\n    const ipInfo = parseIpAddress(currentIp);\n    const oldIpInfo = parseIpAddress(session.clientIp);\n\n    if (ipInfo.type !== oldIpInfo.type) {\n      riskScore += 40;\n      riskFactors.push(\"IP address type changed (IPv4 ↔ IPv6)\");\n      events.push({\n        type: \"ip_type_change\",\n        timestamp: now,\n        score: 40,\n        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n      });\n    } else if (!areIpsInSameSubnet(session.clientIp, currentIp, 24)) {\n      riskScore += 30;\n      riskFactors.push(\"IP address changed to different subnet\");\n      events.push({\n        type: \"ip_subnet_change\",\n        timestamp: now,\n        score: 30,\n        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n      });\n    } else {\n      riskScore += 10;\n      riskFactors.push(\"IP address changed within same subnet\");\n      events.push({\n        type: \"ip_change_same_subnet\",\n        timestamp: now,\n        score: 10,\n        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check User-Agent change\n  if (currentUserAgent && session.userAgent && session.userAgent !== currentUserAgent) {\n    const similarity = calculateUserAgentSimilarity(session.userAgent, currentUserAgent);\n\n    if (similarity < 50) {\n      riskScore += 30;\n      riskFactors.push(\"User-Agent changed significantly\");\n      events.push({\n        type: \"ua_change_major\",\n        timestamp: now,\n        score: 30,\n        details: { oldUa: session.userAgent, newUa: currentUserAgent, similarity },\n      });\n    } else if (!isLegitimateUserAgentChange(session.userAgent, currentUserAgent)) {\n      riskScore += 15;\n      riskFactors.push(\"User-Agent changed (possibly suspicious)\");\n      events.push({\n        type: \"ua_change_minor\",\n        timestamp: now,\n        score: 15,\n        details: { oldUa: session.userAgent, newUa: currentUserAgent },\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check session age (very new sessions are higher risk)\n  const sessionAge = now - session.createdAt;\n  if (sessionAge < 5 * 60 * 1000) {\n    // Less than 5 minutes old\n    riskScore += 5;\n    riskFactors.push(\"Very new session\");\n  }\n\n  // Check idle time (long idle may indicate hijacking)\n  const idleTime = now - session.lastActivityAt;\n  if (idleTime > 60 * 60 * 1000) {\n    // More than 1 hour idle\n    riskScore += 10;\n    riskFactors.push(\"Long idle time before activity\");\n  }\n\n  // Include recent security events\n  for (const event of recentEvents) {\n    riskScore += event.score;\n    events.push(event);\n  }\n\n  // Cap risk score at 100\n  riskScore = Math.min(100, riskScore);\n\n  // Determine risk level\n  let riskLevel: \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n  let recommendedAction: \"allow\" | \"monitor\" | \"challenge\" | \"block\";\n\n  if (riskScore < 20) {\n    riskLevel = \"low\";\n    recommendedAction = \"allow\";\n  } else if (riskScore < 50) {\n    riskLevel = \"medium\";\n    recommendedAction = \"monitor\";\n  } else if (riskScore < 80) {\n    riskLevel = \"high\";\n    recommendedAction = \"challenge\";\n  } else {\n    riskLevel = \"critical\";\n    recommendedAction = \"block\";\n  }\n\n  return {\n    riskScore,\n    riskLevel,\n    riskFactors,\n    recommendedAction,\n    events,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Record a security event for a session.\n */\nexport function recordSecurityEvent(sessionId: string, event: SecurityEvent): void {\n  const events = sessionSecurityEvents.get(sessionId) || [];\n  events.push(event);\n\n  // Keep only last 100 events per session\n  if (events.length > 100) {\n    events.shift();\n  }\n\n  sessionSecurityEvents.set(sessionId, events);\n\n  logger.debug(\"Security event recorded\", {\n    sessionId,\n    eventType: event.type,\n    score: event.score,\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Clear security events for a session.\n */\nexport function clearSecurityEvents(sessionId: string): void {\n  sessionSecurityEvents.delete(sessionId);\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Device Trust Management\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Device trust storage.\n */\nconst deviceTrustStorage = new Map<string, DeviceTrustInfo>();\n\n/**\n * Get or create device trust info.\n */\nexport function getOrCreateDeviceTrust(\n  deviceId: string,\n  initialRiskAssessment?: SessionRiskAssessment\n): DeviceTrustInfo {\n  const existing = deviceTrustStorage.get(deviceId);\n\n  if (existing) {\n    return existing;\n  }\n\n  const trustInfo: DeviceTrustInfo = {\n    deviceId,\n    trustLevel: \"unknown\",\n    firstSeenAt: Date.now(),\n    lastSeenAt: Date.now(),\n    successfulAuths: 0,\n    lastRiskAssessment: initialRiskAssessment,\n  };\n\n  deviceTrustStorage.set(deviceId, trustInfo);\n\n  logger.info(\"New device trust record created\", { deviceId });\n\n  return trustInfo;\n}\n\n/**\n * Update device trust based on successful authentication.\n */\nexport function updateDeviceTrust(deviceId: string, riskAssessment?: SessionRiskAssessment): void {\n  const trustInfo = getOrCreateDeviceTrust(deviceId, riskAssessment);\n\n  // Ensure lastSeenAt is always at least 1ms after firstSeenAt\n  const now = Date.now();\n  trustInfo.lastSeenAt = Math.max(now, trustInfo.firstSeenAt + 1);\n  trustInfo.successfulAuths++;\n  trustInfo.lastRiskAssessment = riskAssessment;\n\n  // Auto-promote trust based on successful auths\n  if (trustInfo.successfulAuths >= 10 && trustInfo.trustLevel !== \"highly_trusted\") {\n    trustInfo.trustLevel = \"highly_trusted\";\n    logger.info(\"Device promoted to highly trusted\", { deviceId });\n  } else if (trustInfo.successfulAuths >= 5 && trustInfo.trustLevel === \"unknown\") {\n    trustInfo.trustLevel = \"trusted\";\n    logger.info(\"Device promoted to trusted\", { deviceId });\n  } else if (riskAssessment && riskAssessment.riskLevel === \"high\") {\n    trustInfo.trustLevel = \"untrusted\";\n    logger.warn(\"Device marked as untrusted due to high risk\", { deviceId });\n  }\n\n  deviceTrustStorage.set(deviceId, trustInfo);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a device is trusted.\n */\nexport function isDeviceTrusted(deviceId: string): boolean {\n  const trustInfo = deviceTrustStorage.get(deviceId);\n  return trustInfo?.trustLevel === \"trusted\" || trustInfo?.trustLevel === \"highly_trusted\";\n}\n\n/**\n * Get device trust level.\n */\nexport function getDeviceTrustLevel(deviceId: string): DeviceTrustLevel {\n  return deviceTrustStorage.get(deviceId)?.trustLevel || \"unknown\";\n}\n\n/**\n * Manually set device trust level.\n */\nexport function setDeviceTrustLevel(deviceId: string, trustLevel: DeviceTrustLevel): void {\n  const trustInfo = getOrCreateDeviceTrust(deviceId);\n  trustInfo.trustLevel = trustLevel;\n  deviceTrustStorage.set(deviceId, trustInfo);\n\n  logger.info(\"Device trust level manually set\", { deviceId, trustLevel });\n}\n\n/**\n * Remove device trust record.\n */\nexport function removeDeviceTrust(deviceId: string): void {\n  deviceTrustStorage.delete(deviceId);\n  logger.info(\"Device trust record removed\", { deviceId });\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Impossible Travel Detection\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Maximum plausible travel speed (km/h).\n * Commercial jets fly at ~900 km/h, so anything faster is suspicious.\n */\nconst MAX_PLAUSIBLE_SPEED_KMH = 900;\n\n/**\n * Check for impossible travel between two locations.\n *\n * Compares the time between two events with the distance between\n * their locations to detect impossible travel scenarios.\n */\nexport async function detectImpossibleTravel(\n  event1: { timestamp: number; ip: string; geo?: GeolocationData },\n  event2: { timestamp: number; ip: string; geo?: GeolocationData },\n  options: {\n    /** Maximum plausible speed (km/h) */\n    maxSpeedKmh?: number;\n    /** Geolocation lookup function */\n    geoLookup?: (ip: string) => Promise<GeolocationData | null>;\n  } = {}\n): Promise<{ impossible: boolean; speed?: number; distance?: number }> {\n  const { maxSpeedKmh = MAX_PLAUSIBLE_SPEED_KMH, geoLookup } = options;\n\n  // Ensure event1 is earlier\n  const [earlier, later] =\n    event1.timestamp < event2.timestamp ? [event1, event2] : [event2, event1];\n\n  // Get geolocation data\n  const geo1 = event1.geo || (geoLookup ? await geoLookup(event1.ip) : null);\n  const geo2 = event2.geo || (geoLookup ? await geoLookup(event2.ip) : null);\n\n  if (!geo1 || !geo2 || !geo1.latitude || !geo1.longitude || !geo2.latitude || !geo2.longitude) {\n    // Can't determine without geolocation\n    return { impossible: false };\n  }\n\n  // Calculate distance using Haversine formula\n  const distance = calculateDistance(geo1.latitude, geo1.longitude, geo2.latitude, geo2.longitude);\n\n  // Calculate time difference in hours\n  const timeDiffHours = (later.timestamp - earlier.timestamp) / (1000 * 60 * 60);\n\n  if (timeDiffHours <= 0) {\n    // Events at the same time or reversed timestamps\n    return { impossible: false };\n  }\n\n  // Calculate required speed\n  const speed = distance / timeDiffHours;\n\n  if (speed > maxSpeedKmh) {\n    return { impossible: true, speed, distance };\n  }\n\n  return { impossible: false, speed, distance };\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate distance between two coordinates using the Haversine formula.\n *\n * Returns distance in kilometers.\n */\nexport function calculateDistance(lat1: number, lon1: number, lat2: number, lon2: number): number {\n  const R = 6371; // Earth's radius in km\n  const dLat = toRadians(lat2 - lat1);\n  const dLon = toRadians(lon2 - lon1);\n\n  const a =\n    Math.sin(dLat / 2) * Math.sin(dLat / 2) +\n    Math.cos(toRadians(lat1)) * Math.cos(toRadians(lat2)) * Math.sin(dLon / 2) * Math.sin(dLon / 2);\n\n  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a));\n\n  return R * c;\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert degrees to radians.\n */\nfunction toRadians(degrees: number): number {\n  return degrees * (Math.PI / 180);\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Middleware Integration\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Session security middleware options.\n */\nexport interface SessionSecurityMiddlewareOptions {\n  /** Whether to enforce IP binding */\n  enforceIpBinding?: boolean;\n  /** Whether to check user agent changes */\n  checkUserAgent?: boolean;\n  /** Risk threshold for blocking (0-100) */\n  riskThreshold?: number;\n  /** Whether to require re-authentication on high risk */\n  reauthOnHighRisk?: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Session security middleware.\n *\n * Enhances session validation with risk assessment and additional security checks.\n */\nexport function sessionSecurity(options: SessionSecurityMiddlewareOptions = {}) {\n  const {\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    enforceIpBinding = true,\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    checkUserAgent = true,\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    riskThreshold = 80,\n    reauthOnHighRisk = true,\n  } = options;\n\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any\n  return async (c: any, next: any) => {\n    const session = c.get(\"session\");\n    if (!session) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    const clientIp =\n      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n      \"unknown\";\n\n    const userAgent = c.req.header(\"User-Agent\");\n\n    // Assess session risk\n    const riskAssessment = await assessSessionRisk(session, clientIp, userAgent);\n\n    // Attach risk assessment to context\n    c.set(\"sessionRiskAssessment\", riskAssessment);\n\n    // Take action based on risk level\n    if (riskAssessment.recommendedAction === \"block\") {\n      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n        c,\n        \"high_risk_session_blocked\",\n        `Session blocked due to high risk score: ${riskAssessment.riskScore}`\n      );\n      throw new Error(\"Session blocked due to suspicious activity\");\n    }\n\n    if (riskAssessment.recommendedAction === \"challenge\" && reauthOnHighRisk) {\n      // Require re-authentication for high-risk sessions\n      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n        c,\n        \"high_risk_session_challenge\",\n        `Re-authentication required due to risk score: ${riskAssessment.riskScore}`\n      );\n      throw new Error(\"Re-authentication required\");\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n","numLines":891,"startLine":1,"totalLines":891}}}

PasswordResetToken>();\n1099\tconst passwordHistory = new Map<string, PasswordHistoryEntry[]>();\n1100\tconst passwordValidationAttempts = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();\n1101\t\n1102\t/**\n1103\t * Password reset attempt tracking.\n1104\t * Tracks failed reset attempts per email/IP combination to prevent brute force.\n1105\t * Key format: \"email:ip\" or }\n1110\t>();\n1111\t\n1112\t/**\n1113\t * Account lockout tracking.\n1114\t * Tracks accounts that are temporarily locked due to suspicious activity.\n1115\t */\n1116\tconst accountLockouts = new tokenCleanupInterval: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;\n1125\t\n1126\t/**\n1127\t * Breached password cache to reduce API calls.\n1128\t * Stores password hash prefixes that have been for breached password results (1 hour).\n1137\t * Passwords that change breach status frequently should be re-checked.\n1138\t */\n1139\tconst BREACHED_PASSWORD_CACHE_TTL_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000;\n1140\t\n1141\t/**\n1142\t * Pepper for additional password security.\n1143\t * In production, this should be stored securely (e.g., in a secrets manager)\n1144\t * and loaded via environment variable or secure configuration.\n1145\t *\n1146\t * The pepper is a server-wide secret that is combined with the password\n1147\t * before hashing. This provides defense in depth even if the database\n1148\t * is compromised, as the attacker would also need the pepper.\n1149\t *\n1150\t * @see https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Password_Storage_Cheat_Sheet.html\n1151\t */\n1152\tlet PASSWORD_PEPPER = \"\";\n1153\t\n1154\t/**\n1155\t * Set the password pepper for additional security.\n1156\t *\n1157\t * IMPORTANT: In production, this should be loaded from a secure source\n1158\t * like a secrets manager or environment variable. Never hardcode the pepper.\n1159\t *\n1160\t * @param pepper - The pepper value to use (hex encoded string), or empty string to disable\n1161\t */\n1162\texport function setPasswordPepper(pepper: string): void {\n1163\t  // Allow empty string to disable pepper\n1164\t  if (pepper === \"\") {\n1165\t    PASSWORD_PEPPER = \"\";\n1166\t    logger.info(\"Password pepper disabled\");\n1167\t    return;\n1168\t  }\n1169\t\n1170\t  // Validate pepper is a valid hex string\n1171\t  if (!/^[a-fA-F0-9]+$/.test(pepper)) {\n1172\t    throw new Error(\"Password pepper must be a hex string\");\n1173\t  }\n1174\t  if (pepper.length < 32) {\n1175\t    throw new Error(\"Password pepper must be at least 16 bytes (32 hex chars)\");\n1176\t  }\n1177\t  PASSWORD_PEPPER = pepper;\n1178\t  logger.info(\"Password pepper configured\");\n1179\t}\n1180\t\n1181\t/**\n1182\t * Get the current password pepper.\n1183\t * Returns an empty string if not configured.\n1184\t *\n1185\t * @internal\n1186\t */\n1187\tfunction getPasswordPepper(): string {\n1188\t  return PASSWORD_PEPPER;\n1189\t}\n1190\t\n1191\t// ============================================================================\n1192\t// Password Reset Attempt Tracking & Account Lockout\n1193\t// ============================================================================\n1194\t\n1195\t/**\n1196\t * Check if an account is currently locked due to too many failed reset attempts.\n1197\t *\n1198\t * @param email - The email address to check\n1199\t * @returns Object indicating if locked and when it will be unlocked\n1200\t */\n1201\texport function isAccountLocked(email: string): {\n1202\t  locked: boolean;\n1203\t  reason?: string;\n1204\t  unlockTime?: number;\n1205\t  remainingMinutes?: number;\n1206\t} {\n1207\t  const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase();\n1208\t  const lockout = accountLockouts.get(normalizedEmail);\n1209\t\n1210\t  if (!lockout) {\n1211\t    return { locked: false };\n1212\t  }\n1213\t\n1214\t  // Check if lockout has expired\n1215\t  if (Date.now() > lockout.lockedUntil) {\n1216\t    accountLockouts.delete(normalizedEmail);\n1217\t    passwordResetAttempts.delete(normalizedEmail);\n1218\t    return { locked: false };\n1219\t  }\n1220\t\n1221\t  const remainingMinutes = Math.ceil((lockout.lockedUntil - Date.now()) / (60 * 1000));\n1222\t\n1223\t  return {\n1224\t    locked: true,\n1225\t    reason: lockout.reason,\n1226\t    unlockTime: lockout.lockedUntil,\n1227\t    remainingMinutes,\n1228\t  };\n1229\t}\n1230\t\n1231\t/**\n1232\t * Record a failed password reset attempt.\n1233\t * Tracks attempts and implements account lockout after too many failures.\n1234\t *\n1235\t * @param email - The email address\n1236\t * @param clientIp - The client IP address\n1237\t * @returns Object indicating if account is now locked and details\n1238\t */\n1239\texport function recordFailedResetAttempt(\n1240\t  email: string,\n1241\t  clientIp: string\n1242\t): {\n1243\t  locked: boolean;\n1244\t  attemptCount: number;\n1245\t  remainingAttempts: number;\n1246\t  lockedUntil?: number;\n1247\t} {\n1248\t  const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase();\n1249\t  const key = `${normalizedEmail}:${clientIp}`;\n1250\t  const now = Date.now();\n1251\t\n1252\t  let attempt = passwordResetAttempts.get(key);\n1253\t\n1254\t  // Reset if window has expired\n1255\t  if (!attempt || now > attempt.resetAt) {\n1256\t    attempt = { count: 0, resetAt: now + RESET_ATTEMPT_WINDOW_MS };\n1257\t  }\n1258\t\n1259\t  attempt.count++;\n1260\t  passwordResetAttempts.set(key, attempt);\n1261\t\n1262\t  // Also track IP-agnostic attempts (same email from any IP)\n1263\t  let globalAttempt = passwordResetAttempts.get(normalizedEmail);\n1264\t  if (!globalAttempt || now > globalAttempt.resetAt) {\n1265\t    globalAttempt = { count: 0, resetAt: now + RESET_ATTEMPT_WINDOW_MS };\n1266\t  }\n1267\t  globalAttempt.count++;\n1268\t  passwordResetAttempts.set(normalizedEmail, globalAttempt);\n1269\t\n1270\t  const remainingAttempts = Math.max(0, MAX_RESET_ATTEMPTS - globalAttempt.count);\n1271\t\n1272\t  // Check if we should lock the account\n1273\t  if (globalAttempt.count >= MAX_RESET_ATTEMPTS) {\n1274\t    const lockedUntil = now + ACCOUNT_LOCKOUT_DURATION_MS;\n1275\t    accountLockouts.set(normalizedEmail, {\n1276\t      lockedUntil,\n1277\t      reason: \"Too many failed password reset attempts\",\n1278\t      attempts: globalAttempt.count,\n1279\t    });\n1280\t\n1281\t    logger.warn(\"Account locked due to too many failed reset attempts\", {\n1282\t      email: normalizedEmail,\n1283\t      clientIp,\n1284\t      attempts: globalAttempt.count,\n1285\t      lockedUntil: new Date(lockedUntil).toISOString(),\n1286\t    });\n1287\t\n1288\t    return {\n1289\t      locked: true,\n1290\t      attemptCount: globalAttempt.count,\n1291\t      remainingAttempts: 0,\n1292\t      lockedUntil,\n1293\t    };\n1294\t  }\n1295\t\n1296\t  return {\n1297\t    locked: false,\n1298\t    attemptCount: globalAttempt.count,\n1299\t    remainingAttempts,\n1300\t  };\n1301\t}\n1302\t\n1303\t/**\n1304\t * Clear failed reset attempts for an email (e.g., after successful reset).\n1305\t *\n1306\t * @param email - The email address\n1307\t */\n1308\texport function clearFailedResetAttempts(email: string): void {\n1309\t  const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase();\n1310\t\n1311\t  // Remove all entries that start with this email\n1312\t  for (const key of passwordResetAttempts.keys()) {\n1313\t    if (key === normalizedEmail || key.startsWith(`${normalizedEmail}:`)) {\n1314\t      passwordResetAttempts.delete(key);\n1315\t    }\n1316\t  }\n1317\t\n1318\t  // Also remove from lockouts\n1319\t  accountLockouts.delete(normalizedEmail);\n1320\t}\n1321\t\n1322\t/**\n1323\t * Get current failed reset attempt count for an email/IP combination.\n1324\t *\n1325\t * @param email - The email address\n1326\t * @param clientIp - The client IP address\n1327\t * @returns Number of failed attempts\n1328\t */\n1329\texport function getFailedResetAttemptCount(email: string, clientIp: string): number {\n1330\t  const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase();\n1331\t  const key = `${normalizedEmail}:${clientIp}`;\n1332\t  const attempt = passwordResetAttempts.get(key);\n1333\t\n1334\t  if (!attempt || Date.now() > attempt.resetAt) {\n1335\t    return 0;\n1336\t  }\n1337\t\n1338\t  return attempt.count;\n1339\t}\n1340\t\n1341\t/**\n1342\t * Start the automatic token cleanup interval.\n1343\t * Removes expired tokens periodically to prevent memory leaks.\n1344\t *\n1345\t * @internal\n1346\t */\n1347\texport function _startTokenCleanup(): void {\n1348\t  if (tokenCleanupInterval) {\n1349\t    return; // Already running\n1350\t  }\n1351\t\n1352\t  tokenCleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {\n1353\t    const now = Date.now();\n1354\t    let cleaned = 0;\n1355\t\n1356\t    for (const [tokenId, tokenData] of passwordResetTokens.entries()) {\n1357\t      // Remove expired or used tokens\n1358\t      if (now > tokenData.expiresAt || tokenData.used) {\n1359\t        passwordResetTokens.delete(tokenId);\n1360\t        cleaned++;\n1361\t      }\n1362\t    }\n1363\t\n1364\t    // Clean expired lockouts\n1365\t    for (const [email, lockout] of accountLockouts.entries()) {\n1366\t      if (now > lockout.lockedUntil) {\n1367\t        accountLockouts.delete(email);\n1368\t        passwordResetAttempts.delete(email);\n1369\t      }\n1370\t    }\n1371\t\n1372\t    if (cleaned > 0) {\n1373\t      logger.debug(\"Token cleanup completed\", { cleaned, remaining: passwordResetTokens.size });\n1374\t    }\n1375\t  }, TOKEN_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS);\n1376\t\n1377\t  logger.info(\"Token cleanup interval started\", { intervalMs: TOKEN_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS });\n1378\t}\n1379\t\n1380\t/**\n1381\t * Stop the automatic token cleanup interval.\n1382\t *\n1383\t * @internal\n1384\t */\n1385\texport function _stopTokenCleanup(): void {\n1386\t  if (tokenCleanupInterval) {\n1387\t    clearInterval(tokenCleanupInterval);\n1388\t    tokenCleanupInterval = null;\n1389\t    logger.info(\"Token cleanup interval stopped\");\n1390\t  }\n1391\t}\n1392\t\n1393\t/**\n1394\t * Manually trigger token cleanup.\n1395\t * Useful for testing or immediate cleanup.\n1396\t *\n1397\t * @returns Number of tokens cleaned up\n1398\t */\n1399\texport function cleanupExpiredTokens(): number {\n1400\t  const now = Date.now();\n1401\t  let cleaned = 0;\n1402\t\n1403\t  for (const [tokenId, tokenData] of passwordResetTokens.entries()) {\n1404\t    if (now > tokenData.expiresAt || tokenData.used) {\n1405\t      passwordResetTokens.delete(tokenId);\n1406\t      cleaned++;\n1407\t    }\n1408\t  }\n1409\t\n1410\t  // Clean expired lockouts\n1411\t  for (const [email, lockout] of accountLockouts.entries()) {\n1412\t    if (now > lockout.lockedUntil) {\n1413\t      accountLockouts.delete(email);\n1414\t      passwordResetAttempts.delete(email);\n1415\t    }\n1416\t  }\n1417\t\n1418\t  return cleaned;\n1419\t}\n1420\t\n1421\t// ============================================================================\n1422\t// Password Validation\n1423\t// ============================================================================\n1424\t\n1425\t/**\n1426\t * Detect sequential character patterns in password.\n1427\t * Checks for:\n1428\t * - Numeric sequences: 1234, 4321, 0123\n1429\t * - Alphabetic sequences: abcd, DCBA, mnop\n1430\t * - Both forward and reverse sequences\n1431\t *\n1432\t * @param password - The password to check\n1433\t * @param maxSequence - Maximum allowed sequential characters\n1434\t * @returns Object indicating if sequential pattern was found and the pattern\n1435\t */\n1436\tfunction detectSequentialChars(\n1437\t  password: string,\n1438\t  maxSequence: number\n1439\t): { found: boolean; pattern?: string } {\n1440\t  const lowerPassword = password.toLowerCase();\n1441\t\n1442\t  // Check each position for sequences\n1443\t  for (let i = 0; i < lowerPassword.length - (maxSequence - 1); i++) {\n1444\t    // Extract potential sequence\n1445\t    const sequence = lowerPassword.substring(i, i + maxSequence + 1);\n1446\t\n1447\t    // Check if it's a valid sequence (all letters or all digits)\n1448\t    const isAllLetters = /^[a-z]+$/.test(sequence);\n1449\t    const isAllDigits = /^\\d+$/.test(sequence);\n1450\t\n1451\t    if (!isAllLetters && !isAllDigits) {\n1452\t      continue;\n1453\t    }\n1454\t\n1455\t    // Check for forward or reverse sequential pattern\n1456\t    if (isSequentialSequence(sequence)) {\n1457\t      return { found: true, pattern: sequence };\n1458\t    }\n1459\t  }\n1460\t\n1461\t  return { found: false };\n1462\t}\n1463\t\n1464\t/**\n1465\t * Check if a string is a sequential character pattern.\n1466\t * Handles both forward (abcd, 1234) and reverse (dcba, 4321) sequences.\n1467\t */\n1468\tfunction isSequentialSequence(str: string): boolean {\n1469\t  if (str.length < 2) return false;\n1470\t\n1471\t  // Check forward sequence\n1472\t  let isForward = true;\n1473\t  let isReverse = true;\n1474\t\n1475\t  for (let i = 0; i < str.length - 1; i++) {\n1476\t    const current = str.charCodeAt(i);\n1477\t    const next = str.charCodeAt(i + 1);\n1478\t\n1479\t    // Forward: next char is exactly +1\n1480\t    if (next !== current + 1) {\n1481\t      isForward = false;\n1482\t    }\n1483\t\n1484\t    // Reverse: next char is exactly -1\n1485\t    if (next !== current - 1) {\n1486\t      isReverse = false;\n1487\t    }\n1488\t  }\n1489\t\n1490\t  return isForward || isReverse;\n1491\t}\n1492\t\n1493\t/**\n1494\t * Detect keyboard walking patterns in password.\n1495\t * Checks for common keyboard sequences like \"qwerty\", \"asdf\", \"1qaz\", etc.\n1496\t *\n1497\t * Only reports patterns of 3+ characters to avoid false positives on\n1498\t * single characters that happen to be part of keyboard layouts.\n1499\t *\n1500\t * @param password - The password to check\n1501\t * @returns Object indicating if keyboard pattern was found and the pattern\n1502\t */\n1503\tfunction detectKeyboardPatterns(password: string): { found: boolean; pattern?: string } {\n1504\t  const lowerPassword = password.toLowerCase();\n1505\t  const MIN_PATTERN_LENGTH = 3;\n1506\t\n1507\t  // Check each pattern\n1508\t  for (const pattern of KEYBOARD_PATTERNS) {\n1509\t    // Skip patterns shorter than minimum length\n1510\t    if (pattern.length < MIN_PATTERN_LENGTH) {\n1511\t      continue;\n1512\t    }\n1513\t\n1514\t    // Check if pattern exists in password (case-insensitive)\n1515\t    if (lowerPassword.includes(pattern)) {\n1516\t      return { found: true, pattern };\n1517\t    }\n1518\t\n1519\t    // Also check reversed version of pattern\n1520\t    const reversed = pattern.split(\"\").reverse().join(\"\");\n1521\t    if (lowerPassword.includes(reversed)) {\n1522\t      return { found: true, pattern: reversed };\n1523\t    }\n1524\t  }\n1525\t\n1526\t  return { found: false };\n1527\t}\n1528\t\n1529\t/**\n1530\t * Detect common password variations like \"password1\", \"password2\", etc.\n1531\t *\n1532\t * @param password - The password to check\n1533\t * @param skipKeyboardBased - Whether to skip keyboard-based common words (when blockKeyboardPatterns is false)\n1534\t * @returns Object indicating if common variation was found\n1535\t */\n1536\tfunction detectCommonVariations(\n1537\t  password: string,\n1538\t  skipKeyboardBased = false\n1539\t): { found: boolean; pattern?: string } {\n1540\t  const lowerPassword = password.toLowerCase();\n1541\t\n1542\t  // Common base passwords with number/special char variations\n1543\t  // Keyboard-based words (qwerty, asdf) are skipped when blockKeyboardPatterns is false\n1544\t  const commonBases = [\n1545\t    \"password\",\n1546\t    \"passw0rd\",\n1547\t    \"admin\",\n1548\t    \"welcome\",\n1549\t    \"login\",\n1550\t    // Skip keyboard-based words when requested\n1551\t    ...(skipKeyboardBased ? [] : [\"qwerty\"]),\n1552\t    \"letmein\",\n1553\t    \"monkey\",\n1554\t    \"dragon\",\n1555\t    \"master\",\n1556\t  ];\n1557\t\n1558\t  for (const base of commonBases) {\n1559\t    // Check if password starts with common base\n1560\t    if (!lowerPassword.startsWith(base)) {\n1561\t      continue;\n1562\t    }\n1563\t\n1564\t    const suffix = lowerPassword.slice(base.length);\n1565\t\n1566\t    // If there's no suffix, it's just the base word (handled elsewhere)\n1567\t    if (suffix.length === 0) {\n1568\t      continue;\n1569\t    }\n1570\t\n1571\t    // Check if suffix is only numbers\n1572\t    if (/^\\d+$/.test(suffix)) {\n1573\t      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[numbers]\" };\n1574\t    }\n1575\t\n1576\t    // Check if suffix is only special characters\n1577\t    if (/^[!@#$%^&*]+$/.test(suffix)) {\n1578\t      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[special]\" };\n1579\t    }\n1580\t\n1581\t    // Check if suffix is a year (19xx or 20xx)\n1582\t    if (/^(19\\d\\d|20\\d\\d)$/.test(suffix)) {\n1583\t      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[year]\" };\n1584\t    }\n1585\t\n1586\t    // Check if suffix is numbers followed by special chars (e.g., \"password123!@#\")\n1587\t    if (/^\\d+[!@#$%^&*]+$/.test(suffix)) {\n1588\t      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[numbers+special]\" };\n1589\t    }\n1590\t\n1591\t    // Check if suffix is special chars followed by numbers (e.g., \"password!@#123\")\n1592\t    if (/^[!@#$%^&*]+\\d+$/.test(suffix)) {\n1593\t      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[special+numbers]\" };\n1594\t    }\n1595\t\n1596\t    // Check if suffix is a year followed by special chars (e.g., \"password2024!@#\")\n1597\t    if (/^(19\\d\\d|20\\d\\d)[!@#$%^&*]+$/.test(suffix)) {\n1598\t      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[year+special]\" };\n1599\t    }\n1600\t  }\n1601\t\n1602\t  return { found: false };\n1603\t}\n1604\t\n1605\t/**\n1606\t * Validate a password against policy requirements.\n1607\t *\n1608\t * This function performs comprehensive password validation including:\n1609\t * - Length requirements\n1610\t * - Character complexity\n1611\t * - Common password detection\n1612\t * - Character repetition\n1613\t * - Breached password checking (via HIBP k-anonymity API)\n1614\t * - Password strength scoring\n1615\t *\n1616\t * @param password - The password to validate\n1617\t * @param policy - Optional custom policy (uses defaults if not provided)\n1618\t * @param keyId - Optional key ID for password history checking\n1619\t * @returns Validation result with detailed feedback\n1620\t */\n1621\texport async function validatePassword(\n1622\t  password: string,\n1623\t  policy: PasswordPolicy = {},\n1624\t  keyId?: string\n1625\t): Promise<PasswordValidationResult> {\n1626\t  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n1627\t  const errors: string[] = [];\n1628\t\n1629\t  // Check maximum length FIRST before any sanitization\n1630\t  // This ensures we catch long passwords and provide a clear error message\n1631\t  if (password.length > mergedPolicy.maxLength) {\n1632\t    errors.push(`Password must be no more than ${mergedPolicy.maxLength} characters long`);\n1633\t    return {\n1634\t      valid: false,\n1635\t      errors,\n1636\t      strength: 0,\n1637\t      strengthCategory: \"weak\",\n1638\t    };\n1639\t  }\n1640\t\n1641\t  // Check minimum length\n1642\t  if (password.length < mergedPolicy.minLength) {\n1643\t    errors.push(`Password must be at least ${mergedPolicy.minLength} characters long`);\n1644\t  }\n1645\t\n1646\t  // Sanitize input to prevent injection attacks (without length truncation since we already checked)\n1647\t  // For passwords, we only do basic sanitization - no SQL/Command injection prevention\n1648\t  // since those would incorrectly reject valid passwords containing words like \"or\", \"and\", etc.\n1649\t  const sanitizedPassword = sanitizeStringSimple(password, {\n1650\t    maxLength: password.length, // Don't truncate, we already validated length\n1651\t    preserveCase: true,\n1652\t    preserveWhitespace: mergedPolicy.allowSpaces,\n1653\t    preventSqlInjection: false, // Don't prevent SQL injection in passwords\n1654\t    preventCommandInjection: false, // Don't prevent command injection in passwords\n1655\t    preventLdapInjection: false, // Don't prevent LDAP injection in passwords\n1656\t    preventNosqlInjection: false, // Don't prevent NoSQL injection in passwords\n1657\t  });\n1658\t\n1659\t  // Check if sanitization changed the password (injection patterns detected)\n1660\t  // We only check for XSS patterns in passwords since those are actual security risks\n1661\t  if (sanitizedPassword !== password) {\n1662\t    errors.push(\"Password contains invalid characters\");\n1663\t    return {\n1664\t      valid: false,\n1665\t      errors,\n1666\t      strength: 0,\n1667\t      strengthCategory: \"weak\",\n1668\t    };\n1669\t  }\n1670\t\n1671\t  // Check uppercase\n1672\t  if (mergedPolicy.requireUppercase && !/[A-Z]/.test(password)) {\n1673\t    errors.push(\"Password must contain at least one uppercase letter\");\n1674\t  }\n1675\t\n1676\t  // Check lowercase\n1677\t  if (mergedPolicy.requireLowercase && !/[a-z]/.test(password)) {\n1678\t    errors.push(\"Password must contain at least one lowercase letter\");\n1679\t  }\n1680\t\n1681\t  // Check numbers\n1682\t  if (mergedPolicy.requireNumbers && !/\\d/.test(password)) {\n1683\t    errors.push(\"Password must contain at least one number\");\n1684\t  }\n1685\t\n1686\t  // Check special characters\n1687\t  if (mergedPolicy.requireSpecialChars && !/[!@#$%^&*()_+\\-=[\\]{};':\"\\\\|,.<>/?]/.test(password)) {\n1688\t    errors.push(\"Password must contain at least one special character\");\n1689\t  }\n1690\t\n1691\t  // Check spaces\n1692\t  if (!mergedPolicy.allowSpaces && /\\s/.test(password)) {\n1693\t    errors.push(\"Password cannot contain spaces\");\n1694\t  }\n1695\t\n1696\t  // Check against blocked passwords (exact match only)\n1697\t  // Substring matching is too aggressive and would reject valid passwords\n1698\t  // like \"SecurePass123!\" that happen to contain common words\n1699\t  const lowerPassword = password.toLowerCase();\n1700\t  for (const blocked of mergedPolicy.blockedPasswords) {\n1701\t    if (lowerPassword === blocked) {\n1702\t      errors.push(\"Password is too common or weak\");\n1703\t      break;\n1704\t    }\n1705\t  }\n1706\t\n1707\t  // Check character repetition\n1708\t  if (mergedPolicy.maxRepetition > 0) {\n1709\t    const repetitionRegex = new RegExp(`(.)\\\\1{${mergedPolicy.maxRepetition},}`, \"g\");\n1710\t    if (repetitionRegex.test(password)) {\n1711\t      errors.push(\n1712\t        `Password cannot contain the same character more than ${mergedPolicy.maxRepetition} times in a row`\n1713\t      );\n1714\t    }\n1715\t  }\n1716\t\n1717\t  // Check sequential character patterns\n1718\t  if (mergedPolicy.blockSequentialChars && mergedPolicy.maxSequentialChars > 0) {\n1719\t    const sequentialResult = detectSequentialChars(password, mergedPolicy.maxSequentialChars);\n1720\t    if (sequentialResult.found) {\n1721\t      errors.push(\n1722\t        `Password contains sequential characters (${sequentialResult.pattern}). Avoid using patterns like \"1234\" or \"abcd\".`\n1723\t      );\n1724\t    }\n1725\t  }\n1726\t\n1727\t  // Check keyboard walking patterns\n1728\t  if (mergedPolicy.blockKeyboardPatterns) {\n1729\t    const keyboardResult = detectKeyboardPatterns(password);\n1730\t    if (keyboardResult.found) {\n1731\t      errors.push(\n1732\t        `Password contains keyboard pattern (${keyboardResult.pattern}). Avoid using keyboard sequences.`\n1733\t      );\n1734\t    }\n1735\t  }\n1736\t\n1737\t  // Check common password variations\n1738\t  // Skip keyboard-based words when blockKeyboardPatterns is disabled\n1739\t  const variationResult = detectCommonVariations(password, !mergedPolicy.blockKeyboardPatterns);\n1740\t  if (variationResult.found) {\n1741\t    errors.push(\n1742\t      `Password contains a common variation pattern (${variationResult.pattern}). Please choose a more unique password.`\n1743\t    );\n1744\t  }\n1745\t\n1746\t  // Calculate strength score\n1747\t  const strength = calculatePasswordStrength(password);\n1748\t  const strengthCategory = getStrengthCategory(strength);\n1749\t\n1750\t  // Check password history if keyId provided\n1751\t  if (keyId && mergedPolicy.passwordHistoryCount > 0) {\n1752\t    const history = passwordHistory.get(keyId);\n1753\t    if (history) {\n1754\t      // Check against recent passwords\n1755\t      const recentHistory = history.slice(-mergedPolicy.passwordHistoryCount);\n1756\t      for (const entry of recentHistory) {\n1757\t        if (await verifyPasswordHash(password, entry.hash, entry.salt)) {\n1758\t          errors.push(\"Cannot reuse a recent password\");\n1759\t          break;\n1760\t        }\n1761\t      }\n1762\t    }\n1763\t  }\n1764\t\n1765\t  // Check breached passwords via HIBP API\n1766\t  let breached = false;\n1767\t  let breachCount = 0;\n1768\t  if (mergedPolicy.checkBreachedPasswords && errors.length === 0) {\n1769\t    const breachResult = await checkBreachedPassword(password);\n1770\t    breached = breachResult.breached;\n1771\t    breachCount = breachResult.count;\n1772\t\n1773\t    if (breached && breachCount > (mergedPolicy.maxBreachedCount ?? 0)) {\n1774\t      errors.push(\n1775\t        `This password has been found in data breaches ${breachCount} times. Please choose a different password.`\n1776\t      );\n1777\t    }\n1778\t  }\n1779\t\n1780\t  // Check minimum strength score\n1781\t  if (strength < (mergedPolicy.minStrengthScore ?? 0)) {\n1782\t    errors.push(\n1783\t      `Password is too weak (strength: ${strength}/100). Please choose a stronger password.`\n1784\t    );\n1785\t  }\n1786\t\n1787\t  return {\n1788\t    valid: errors.length === 0,\n1789\t    errors,\n1790\t    strength,\n1791\t    strengthCategory,\n1792\t    breached,\n1793\t    breachCount: breached ? breachCount : undefined,\n1794\t  };\n1795\t}\n1796\t\n1797\t/**\n1798\t * Calculate password strength score (0-100).\n1799\t *\n1800\t * Uses entropy-based calculation with bonuses for:\n1801\t * - Length\n1802\t * - Character variety\n1803\t * - Character distribution\n1804\t */\n1805\tfunction calculatePasswordStrength(password: string): number {\n1806\t  let score = 0;\n1807\t\n1808\t  // Length score (up to 40 points)\n1809\t  score += Math.min(40, password.length * 2);\n1810\t\n1811\t  // Character variety (up to 30 points)\n1812\t  if (/[a-z]/.test(password)) score += 5;\n1813\t  if (/[A-Z]/.test(password)) score += 5;\n1814\t  if (/\\d/.test(password)) score += 5;\n1815\t  if (/[!@#$%^&*()_+\\-=[\\]{};':\"\\\\|,.<>/?]/.test(password)) score += 10;\n1816\t  if (/\\s/.test(password)) score += 5;\n1817\t\n1818\t  // Character distribution (up to 20 points)\n1819\t  const uniqueChars = new Set(password).size;\n1820\t  score += Math.min(20, (uniqueChars / password.length) * 20);\n1821\t\n1822\t  // Complexity bonus (up to 10 points)\n1823\t  const hasMixedCase = /[a-z]/.test(password) && /[A-Z]/.test(password);\n1824\t  const hasNumbers = /\\d/.test(password);\n1825\t\n1826\t  // eslint-disable-next-line no-useless-escape\n1827\t  const hasSpecial = /[!@#$%^&*()_+\\-=\\[\\]{};':\"\\\\|,.<>\\/?]/.test(password);\n1828\t  if (hasMixedCase && hasNumbers && hasSpecial) score += 10;\n1829\t\n1830\t  return Math.min(100, score);\n1831\t}\n1832\t\n1833\t/**\n1834\t * Get strength category from score.\n1835\t */\n1836\tfunction getStrengthCategory(score: number): \"weak\" | \"fair\" | \"good\" | \"strong\" {\n1837\t  if (score < 40) return \"weak\";\n1838\t  if (score < 60) return \"fair\";\n1839\t  if (score < 80) return \"good\";\n1840\t  return \"strong\";\n1841\t}\n1842\t\n1843\t/**\n1844\t * Check if a password has been breached using Have I Been Pwned k-anonymity API.\n1845\t *\n1846\t * Uses the k-anonymity model where only the first 5 characters of the\n1847\t * SHA-256 hash are sent to the API. The full hash never leaves the server.\n1848\t *\n1849\t * Results are cached for 1 hour to reduce API calls and improve performance.\n1850\t *\n1851\t * @param password - The password to check\n1852\t * @returns Object indicating if breached and the count\n1853\t */\n1854\tasync function checkBreachedPassword(\n1855\t  password: string\n1856\t): Promise<{ breached: boolean; count: number }> {\n1857\t  try {\n1858\t    // Calculate SHA-256 hash of password\n1859\t    const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n1860\t    const data = encoder.encode(password);\n1861\t    const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest(\"SHA-256\", data);\n1862\t    const hashArray = Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer));\n1863\t    const hash = hashArray.map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\")).join(\"\");\n1864\t\n1865\t    // Get first 5 characters for API request (cache key)\n1866\t    const prefix = hash.substring(0, 5);\n1867\t    const suffix = hash.substring(5);\n1868\t\n1869\t    // Check cache first\n1870\t    const cached = breachedPasswordCache.get(prefix);\n1871\t    const now = Date.now();\n1872\t    if (cached && now < cached.expiresAt) {\n1873\t      // Check if our specific suffix matches\n1874\t      if (cached.breached) {\n1875\t        return { breached: true, count: cached.count };\n1876\t      }\n1877\t      // If cached as not breached, we still need to check if our suffix is in the list\n1878\t      // because the prefix can have multiple suffixes with different breach counts\n1879\t    }\n1880\t\n1881\t    // Query HIBP API\n1882\t    const response = await fetch(`https://api.pwnedpasswords.com/range/${prefix}`, {\n1883\t      method: \"GET\",\n1884\t      headers: {\n1885\t        \"User-Agent\": \"MTA-My-Way-Server/1.0\",\n1886\t      },\n1887\t    });\n1888\t\n1889\t    if (!response.ok) {\n1890\t      logger.warn(\"Failed to check breached password\", { status: response.status });\n1891\t      return { breached: false, count: 0 };\n1892\t    }\n1893\t\n1894\t    const body = await response.text();\n1895\t    const lines = body.split(\"\\n\");\n1896\t\n1897\t    // Check if our suffix is in the results\n1898\t    for (const line of lines) {\n1899\t      const [lineSuffix, countStr] = line.split(\":\");\n1900\t      if (lineSuffix?.toUpperCase() === suffix.toUpperCase()) {\n1901\t        const count = parseInt(countStr ?? \"0\", 10);\n1902\t\n1903\t        // Cache the result\n1904\t        breachedPasswordCache.set(prefix, {\n1905\t          breached: true,\n1906\t          count,\n1907\t          expiresAt: now + BREACHED_PASSWORD_CACHE_TTL_MS,\n1908\t        });\n1909\t\n1910\t        return { breached: true, count };\n1911\t      }\n1912\t    }\n1913\t\n1914\t    // Cache the not-breached result\n1915\t    breachedPasswordCache.set(prefix, {\n1916\t      breached: false,\n1917\t      count: 0,\n1918\t      expiresAt: now + BREACHED_PASSWORD_CACHE_TTL_MS,\n1919\t    });\n1920\t\n1921\t    return { breached: false, count: 0 };\n1922\t  } catch (error) {\n1923\t    logger.warn(\"Error checking breached password\", error as Error);\n1924\t    return { breached: false, count: 0 };\n1925\t  }\n1926\t}\n1927\t\n1928\t/**\n1929\t * Clear the breached password cache.\n1930\t *\n1931\t * This can be useful for testing or if you want to force fresh checks.\n1932\t */\n1933\texport function clearBreachedPasswordCache(): void {\n1934\t  breachedPasswordCache.clear();\n1935\t  logger.info(\"Breached password cache cleared\");\n1936\t}\n1937\t\n1938\t// ============================================================================\n1939\t// Password Hashing\n1940\t// ============================================================================\n1941\t\n1942\t/**\n1943\t * Hash a password using Argon2id.\n1944\t *\n1945\t * Argon2id is the winner of the Password Hashing Competition (2015)\n1946\t * and is recommended by OWASP for new applications.\n1947\t *\n1948\t * Uses OWASP 2024 recommended parameters:\n1949\t * - Memory: 64 MiB\n1950\t * - Iterations: 3\n1951\t * - Parallelism: 4\n1952\t * - Salt length: 32 bytes\n1953\t * - Output length: 32 bytes\n1954\t *\n1955\t * @param password - The password to hash\n1956\t * @returns Promise containing the hash data\n1957\t */\n1958\texport async function hashPassword(password: string): Promise<PasswordHash> {\n1959\t  // Validate password is not empty\n1960\t  if (!password || password.length === 0) {\n1961\t    throw new Error(\"Password cannot be empty\");\n1962\t  }\n1963\t\n1964\t  // Combine password with pepper for additional security (if configured)\n1965\t  const pepper = getPasswordPepper();\n1966\t  const passwordWithPepper = pepper ? password + pepper : password;\n1967\t\n1968\t  // Hash using Argon2id with OWASP 2024 recommended parameters\n1969\t  // The argon2 package handles salt generation automatically\n1970\t  const hash = await argon2.hash(passwordWithPepper, ARGON2_OPTIONS);\n1971\t\n1972\t  // Extract the salt from the encoded hash\n1973\t  // Argon2 format: $argon2id$v=19$m=65536,t=3,p=4$salt$hash\n1974\t  const parts = hash.split(\"$\");\n1975\t  const salt = parts[4]; // Extract salt from encoded hash\n1976\t\n1977\t  return {\n1978\t    hash,\n1979\t    salt,\n1980\t    algorithm: \"argon2id\",\n1981\t    iterations: ARGON2_OPTIONS.timeCost ?? 3,\n1982\t    memoryCost: ARGON2_OPTIONS.memoryCost,\n1983\t    parallelism: ARGON2_OPTIONS.parallelism,\n1984\t  };\n1985\t}\n1986\t\n1987\t/**\n1988\t * Verify a password against a stored Argon2id hash.\n1989\t *\n1990\t * Uses the argon2 package's built-in verify function which handles\n1991\t * timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks.\n1992\t *\n1993\t * @param password - The password to verify\n1994\t * @param hash - The stored Argon2id hash (encoded string)\n1995\t * @param salt - The salt used for hashing (not needed for Argon2, kept for API compatibility)\n1996\t * @returns Promise indicating if the password matches\n1997\t */\n1998\texport async function verifyPasswordHash(\n1999\t  password: string,\n2000\t  hash: string,\n2001\t  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n2002\t  salt: string\n2003\t): Promise<boolean> {\n2004\t  // Validate inputs\n2005\t  if (!password || password.length === 0) {\n2006\t    return false;\n2007\t  }\n2008\t  if (!hash) {\n2009\t    return false;\n2010\t  }\n2011\t\n2012\t  try {\n2013\t    // Combine password with pepper for verification (must match hashing)\n2014\t    const pepper = getPasswordPepper();\n2015\t    const passwordWithPepper = pepper ? password + pepper : password;\n2016\t\n2017\t    // Use argon2.verify which handles timing-safe comparison\n2018\t    return await argon2.verify(hash, passwordWithPepper);\n2019\t  } catch (error) {\n2020\t    // If verification fails (invalid hash format, etc.), return false\n2021\t    logger.warn(\"Password verification failed\", { error: error as Error });\n2022\t    return false;\n2023\t  }\n2024\t}\n2025\t\n2026\t/**\n2027\t * Store a password in history for a key.\n2028\t *\n2029\t * @param keyId - The key ID\n2030\t * @param hash - The password hash\n2031\t * @param salt - The salt used\n2032\t */\n2033\texport function storePasswordInHistory(keyId: string, hash: string, salt: string): void {\n2034\t  const history = passwordHistory.get(keyId) ?? [];\n2058\t}\n2059\t\n2060\t/**\n2061\t * Clear password history for a key.\n2062\t *\n2063\t * @param keyId - The key ID\n2064\t * @returns Number of entries cleared\n2065\t */\n2066\texport function passwordHistory.delete(keyId);\n2071\t  return count;\n2072\t}\n2073\t\n2074\t// ============================================================================\n2075\t// Password Reset Flow\n2076\t// ============================================================================\n2077\t\n2078\t/**\n2079\t * Rate limit password validation attempts to prevent enumeration attacks.\n2080\t * @private Reserved for future use\n2081\t */\n2082\tfunction _checkPasswordValidationRateLimit(clientIp: string): boolean {\n2083\t  const now = Date.now();\n2084\t  const record = passwordValidationAttempts.get(clientIp);\n2085\t\n2086\t  if (!record || now > record.resetAt) {\n2087\t    passwordValidationAttempts.set(clientIp, {\n2088\t      count: 1,\n2089\t      resetAt: now }\n2097\t\n2098\t  record.count++;\n2099\t  return true;\n2100\t}\n2101\t\n2102\t/**\n2103\t * Generate a device fingerprint from user agent for token verification.\n2104\t */\n2105\tasync function generateDeviceFingerprint(userAgent?: string): Promise<string> {\n2106\t  if (!userAgent) return \"unknown\";\n2107\t  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n2108\t  const data = encoder.encode(userAgent);\n2109\t  const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest(\"SHA-256\", data);\n2110\t  const hashArray = Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer));\n2111\t  return hashArray\n2112\t    .map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\"))\n2113\t    .join(\"\")\n2114\t    .substring(0, 16);\n2115\t}\n2116\t\n2117\t/**\n2118\t * Get user-friendly device information from {\n2126\t    return { deviceType: \"Unknown\", browser: \"Unknown\", os: \"Unknown\" };\n2127\t  }\n2128\t\n2129\t  const ua = userAgent.toLowerCase();\n2130\t\n2131\t  // Detect device type\n2132\t (/firefox/i.test(ua)) {\n2146\t    browser = \"Firefox\";\n2147\t  } else if (/edge|edg/i.test(ua)) {\n2148\t    browser = \"Edge\";\n2149\t  } else if (/opr|opera/i.test(ua)) {\n2150\t    browser = };\n2219\t\n2220\t  // Store token\n2221\t  passwordResetTokens.set(tokenId, tokenData);\n2222\t\n2223\t  const deviceInfo = getDeviceInfo(userAgent);\n2224\t\n2225\t  logger.info(\"Password reset (${originalDevice.deviceType}/${originalDevice.browser}).    return false;\n2331\t  }\n2332\t\n2333\t  // Mark as used\n2334\t  tokenData.used = true;\n2335\t\n2336\t  // Delete after a short delay (for logging purposes)\n2337\t  setTimeout(() => {\n2338\t 0;\n2404\t\n2405\t  if (expirationDays === 0) {\n2406\t    return false; // No expiration\n2407\t  }\n2408\t\n2409\t  const expirationMs = expirationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n2410\t  return Date.now() > passwordSetAt + expirationMs;\n2411\t}\n2412\t\n2413\t/**\n2414\t * Check if a password is in the grace period (expired but can still be used).\n2415\t *\n2416\t * @param passwordSetAt - Timestamp when password was set\n2417\t * @param policy - Optional custom policy\n2418\t * @returns True if password is in grace period\n2419\t */\n2420\texport function isPasswordInGracePeriod(\n2421\t  passwordSetAt: number,\n2422\t  policy: PasswordPolicy = {}\n2423\t): boolean {\n2424\t  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n2425\t  const expirationDays = mergedPolicy.passwordExpirationDays ?? 0;\n2426\t  const gracePeriodDays = mergedPolicy.passwordGracePeriodDays ?? 0;\n2427\t\n2428\t  if (expirationDays === 0) {\n2429\t    return false; // No expiration means no grace period\n2430\t  }\n2431\t\n2432\t  if (gracePeriodDays === 0) {\n2433\t    return false; // No grace period configured\n2434\t  }\n2435\t\n2436\t  const expirationMs = expirationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n2437\t  const gracePeriodMs = gracePeriodDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n2438\t  const now = Date.now();\n2439\t  const expiresAt = passwordSetAt + expirationMs;\n2440\t  const graceEndsAt = expiresAt + gracePeriodMs;\n2441\t\n2442\t  // In grace period if expired but still within grace window\n2443\t  return now > expiresAt && now <= graceEndsAt;\n2444\t}\n2445\t\n2446\t/**\n2447\t * Get the number of days remaining in the grace period.\n2448\t *\n2449\t * @param passwordSetAt - Timestamp when password was set\n2450\t * @param policy - Optional custom policy\n2451\t * @returns Days remaining in grace period, or null if not in grace period\n2452\t */\n2453\texport function getDaysInGracePeriod(\n2454\t  passwordSetAt: number,\n2455\t  policy: PasswordPolicy = {}\n2456\t): number | null {\n2457\t  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n2458\t  const expirationDays = mergedPolicy.passwordExpirationDays ?? 0;\n2459\t  const gracePeriodDays = mergedPolicy.passwordGracePeriodDays ?? 0;\n2460\t\n2461\t  if (expirationDays === 0 || gracePeriodDays === 0) {\n2462\t    return null; // No expiration or grace period\n2463\t  }\n2464\t\n2465\t  const expirationMs = expirationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n2466\t  const gracePeriodMs = gracePeriodDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n2467\t  const now = Date.now();\n2468\t  const expiresAt = passwordSetAt + expirationMs;\n2469\t  const graceEndsAt = expiresAt + gracePeriodMs;\n2470\t\n2471\t  if (now <= expiresAt) {\n2472\t    return null; // Not expired yet\n2473\t  }\n2474\t\n2475\t  if (now > graceEndsAt) {\n2476\t    return 0; // Grace period ended\n2477\t  }\n2478\t\n2479\t  // Calculate remaining days in grace period\n2480\t  const remainingMs = graceEndsAt - now;\n2481\t  return Math.ceil(remainingMs / (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000));\n2482\t}\n2483\t\n2484\t/**\n2485\t * Get days until password expiration.\n2486\t *\n2487\t * @param passwordSetAt - Timestamp when password was set\n2488\t * @param policy - Optional custom policy\n2489\t * @returns Days until expiration, or null if no expiration\n2490\t */\n2491\texport function getDaysUntilExpiration(\n2492\t  passwordSetAt: number,\n2493\t  policy: PasswordPolicy = {}\n2494\t): number | null {\n2495\t  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n2496\t  const expirationDays = mergedPolicy.passwordExpirationDays ?? 0;\n2497\t\n2498\t  if (expirationDays === 0) {\n2499\t    return null; // No expiration\n2500\t  }\n2501\t\n2502\t  const expirationMs = passwordSetAt + expirationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n2503\t  const remainingMs = expirationMs - Date.now();\n2504\t\n2505\t  if (remainingMs <= 0) {\n2506\t    return 0; // Already expired\n2507\t  }\n2508\t\n2509\t  return Math.ceil(remainingMs / (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000));\n2510\t}\n2511\t\n2512\t/**\n2513\t * Check if a {\n2527\t    return false; // No expiration\n2528\t  }\n2529\t  return daysUntil <= warningDays;\n2530\t}\n2531\t\n2532\t// ============================================================================\n2533\t// Utilities\n2534\t// ============================================================================\n2535\t\n2536\t/**\n2537\t * Generate a secure random password.\n2538\t *\n2539\t * @param length - Password length (default: 16)\n2540\t * @param options - Options for password generation\n2541\t * @returns Generated password\n2542\t */\n2543\texport function generateSecurePassword(\n2544\t  length = 16,\n2545\t  options: {\n2546\t    includeUppercase?: boolean;\n2547\t    includeLowercase?: boolean;\n2548\t    includeNumbers?: boolean;\n2549\t    includeSpecialChars?: boolean;\n2550\t    excludeAmbiguous?: boolean; // Exclude 0OIl1\n2551\t  } = {}\n2552\t): string {\n2553\t  const {\n2554\t    includeUppercase = true,\n2555\t    includeLowercase = true,\n2556\t    includeNumbers = true,\n2557\t    includeSpecialChars = true,\n2558\t    excludeAmbiguous = false,\n2559\t  } = options;\n2560\t\n2561\t  const uppercase = \"ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZ\" + (excludeAmbiguous ? \"\" : \"IO\");\n2562\t  const lowercase = \"abcdefghjkmnpqrstuvwxyz\" + (excludeAmbiguous ? \"\" : \"ilo\");\n2563\t  const numbers = \"23456789\" + (excludeAmbiguous ? \"\" : \"01\");\n2564\t  const special = \"!@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{}|;:,.<>?\";\n2565\t\n2566\t  let charset = \"\";\n2567\t  if (includeLowercase) charset += lowercase;\n2568\t  if (includeUppercase) charset += uppercase;\n2569\t  if (includeNumbers) charset += numbers;\n2570\t  if (includeSpecialChars) charset += special;\n2571\t\n2572\t  if (charset.length === 0) {\n2573\t    charset = lowercase + numbers;\n2574\t  }\n2575\t\n2576\t  // Ensure at least one character from each selected type is included\n2577\t  let password = \"\";\n2578\t  const guaranteed: string[] = [];\n2579\t  if (includeLowercase) {\n2580\t    const array = new Uint8Array(1);\n2581\t    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n2582\t    guaranteed.push(lowercase[array[0]! % lowercase.length]!);\n2583\t  }\n2584\t  if (includeUppercase) {\n2585\t    const array = new Uint8Array(1);\n2586\t    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n2587\t    guaranteed.push(uppercase[array[0]! % uppercase.length]!);\n2588\t  }\n2589\t  if (includeNumbers) {\n2590\t    const array = new Uint8Array(1);\n2591\t    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n2592\t    guaranteed.push(numbers[array[0]! % numbers.length]!);\n2593\t  }\n2594\t  if (includeSpecialChars) {\n2595\t    const array = new Uint8Array(1);\n2596\t    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n2597\t    guaranteed.push(special[array[0]! % special.length]!);\n2598\t  }\n2599\t\n2600\t  // Fill the rest randomly\n2601\t  const remainingLength = length - guaranteed.length;\n2602\t  if (remainingLength > 0) {\n2603\t    const array = new Uint8Array(remainingLength);\n2604\t    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n2605\t    for (let i = 0; i < remainingLength; i++) {\n2606\t      password += charset[array[i]! % charset.length];\n2607\t    }\n2608\t  }\n2609\t\n2610\t  // Add guaranteed characters and shuffle\n2611\t  password += guaranteed.join(\"\");\n2612\t  const passwordArray = password.split(\"\");\n2613\t  for (let i = passwordArray.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {\n2614\t    const j = Math.floor(Math.random() * (i + 1));\n2615\t    [passwordArray[i], passwordArray[j]] = [passwordArray[j]!, passwordArray[i]!];\n2616\t  }\n2617\t\n2618\t  return passwordArray.join(\"\");\n2619\t}\n2620\t\n2621\t/**\n2622\t * Get password policy for display to users.\n2623\t *\n2624\t * @returns Formatted password policy requirements\n2625\t */\n2626\texport };\n2650\t}\n2651\t\n2652\t/**\n2653\t * Risk-based password expiration configuration.\n2654\t * Maps user roles to password expiration periods.\n2655\t */\n2656\tconst RISK_BASED_EXPIRATION: guest: 0, // Guest accounts: no expiration\n2661\t};\n2662\t\n2663\t/**\n2664\t * Get password expiration days based on user role.\n2665\t *\n2666\t * This implements risk-based authentication where RISK_BASED_EXPIRATION.user ?? 0;\n2674\t}\n2675\t\n2676\t/**\n2677\t * Get password policy for a specific role.\n2678\t *\n2679\t * Returns the password policy with role-specific expiration * Password Management Module\n *\n * Provides:\n * - Enhanced password policy validation with breach detection\n * - Secure password hashing using Argon2id (industry-standard)\n * - Password reset flow with secure token mechanism\n * - Password expiration and rotation policies\n * - Password history tracking to prevent reuse\n * - Rate limiting for password operations\n *\n * Security Best Practices:\n * - Argon2id with OWASP recommended parameters (2024)\n * - 32-byte cryptographically random salts\n * - Password breach detection using SHA-256 k-anonymity\n * - Secure reset tokens with ============================================================================\n// Password Policy Configuration\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Enhanced password policy requirements following NIST SP 800-63B and OWASP guidelines.\n */\nexport interface PasswordPolicy {\n  /** Minimum password length (default: 12, NIST recommends 8 but we enforce 12 for security) */\n  minLength?: number;\n  /** Maximum password length (default: 128, prevents DoS) */\n  maxLength?: number;\n  /** Require uppercase letters (default: true) */\n  requireUppercase?: boolean;\n  /** Require lowercase letters (default: true) */\n  requireLowercase?: boolean;\n  /** Require numbers (default: true) */\n  requireNumbers?: boolean;\n  /** Require special characters (default: true) */\n  requireSpecialChars?: boolean;\n  /** Blocked common passwords (default: extensive list) */\n  blockedPasswords?: string[];\n  /** Maximum character repetition (default: 3) */\n  maxRepetition?: number;\n  /** Check for breached passwords via Have I Been Pwned API (default: true) */\n  checkBreachedPasswords?: boolean;\n  /** Minimum breach count threshold (default: 0 means any breach fails) */\n  maxBreachedCount?: number;\n  /** Allow spaces in passwords (default: true, NIST recommends allowing) */\n  allowSpaces?: boolean;\n  /** Password age in days before expiration (0 = no expiration) */\n  passwordExpirationDays?: number;\n  /** Number of passwords to remember for history (default: 12) */\n  passwordHistoryCount?: number;\n  /** Block */\n  minStrengthScore?: number;\n  /** Grace period in days after expiration (default: 0, meaning no grace period) */\n  passwordGracePeriodDays?: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Password validation result parallelism?: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Password reset token data.\n */\nexport interface PasswordResetToken {\n  /** Token ID */\n  tokenId: string;\n  /** User/key ID this token is for */\n  keyId:
